tisax.faq.title:
Everything about
TISAX.

faq.items.question: What is ENX Association?
faq.items.answer: The organization founded by major European OEMs (VW, BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Stellantis, Renault) that manages TISAX and the platform where results are registered and shared.

faq.items.question: What TISAX level do I need?
faq.items.answer: Your customer determines it based on the information you share: AL2 is common; AL3 for prototypes and high confidentiality. If in doubt, we'll interpret your customer's requirement for free.

faq.items.question: Is TISAX a certification or an assessment?
faq.items.answer: An assessment: there is no public certificate, but a result on the ENX platform that you share in a controlled manner. A single assessment serves all your customers.

faq.items.question: How long is the TISAX result valid?
faq.items.answer: 3 years, shareable with all customers who request it. Our maintenance package manages the renewal.

faq.items.question: Does ISO 27001 help with TISAX?
faq.items.answer: A lot: overlap exceeds 70%. With ISO 27001 you reach the assessment in 6–8 weeks, compared to 3–4 months from scratch.

faq.items.question: Who performs the assessment? Does Avson do it?
faq.items.answer: No: it is done by an independent auditor accredited by ENX, as required by the scheme. Avson prepares you and supports you throughout the process.

faq.items.question: How much does TISAX cost?
faq.items.answer: Two components: preparation consulting (depending on starting point and level) and ENX assessment fees. Request a quote: full estimate before you commit.

faq.items.question: How is the result shared with OEMs?
faq.items.answer: Digitally on the ENX platform: you authorize which customer can see it. No need to send sensitive documents by email.

privacidad.hero.label: Legal
privacidad.hero.title: Privacy Policy
privacidad.s1.title: 1. Data Controller
privacidad.s1.intro: In compliance with Regulation (EU) 2016/679 of the European Parliament and of the Council (GDPR) and the Spanish Organic Law 3/2018, of 5 December, on Personal Data Protection and guarantee of digital rights (LOPDGDD), we inform you that the controller of your personal data is:
privacidad.s1.company.name: Company name: Avson Consultoría Estratégica SLU
privacidad.s1.company.address: Registered address: Calle Pedro Muñoz Seca 2 (Madrid) · Plaza Francesc Macià 7 (Barcelona), Spain
privacidad.s2.title: 2. Data we collect
privacidad.s2.intro: We collect personal data in the following contexts:
privacidad.s2.contact.title: Contact form and diagnostic request
privacidad.s2.contact.text: First and last name, email address, phone (optional), company name, sector and free-text message. This data is provided voluntarily by the user.
privacidad.s2.newsletter.title: Newsletter
privacidad.s2.newsletter.text: Email address for sending our monthly newsletter on ENS and regulatory compliance.
privacidad.s2.cookies.title: Browsing data and cookies
privacidad.s2.cookies.text: IP address, browser type, operating system, pages visited, time spent and browsing behaviour, through analytical cookies (Google Analytics). See our Cookie Policy for more information.
privacidad.s2.webinars.title: Webinar registration
privacidad.s2.webinars.text: Name, email and company for managing registrations for our free training webinars.
privacidad.s3.title: 3. Purposes of processing and legal basis
privacidad.s3.table.row1.purpose: Respond to queries and no-obligation quote requests
privacidad.s3.table.row2.purpose: Management of commercial relationship and provision of consulting services
privacidad.s3.table.row3.purpose: Sending newsletter with content on ENS and regulatory compliance
privacidad.s3.table.row4.basis: Consent / Performance of a contract (Art. 6.1.a and 6.1.b GDPR)
privacidad.s3.table.row5.purpose: Statistical analysis of website traffic and improvement of user experience
privacidad.s3.table.row6.purpose: Compliance with legal obligations (tax, accounting, commercial)
privacidad.s4.title: 4. Retention periods
privacidad.s4.intro: Personal data will be kept for the periods indicated below:
privacidad.s4.item1: Queries and diagnostic requests: 12 months from the last communication, unless a commercial relationship is initiated.
privacidad.s4.item2: Active clients: For the duration of the contractual relationship and, subsequently, for the applicable statutory limitation periods (generally 5 years for commercial obligations and 4 years for tax obligations).
privacidad.s4.item3: Newsletter: Until the data subject withdraws their consent or requests unsubscription.
privacidad.s4.item4: Webinars: 6 months from the date of the event, unless the participant becomes a client or newsletter subscriber.
privacidad.s4.item5: Browsing data (analytical cookies): According to the periods configured in Google Analytics (maximum 14 months).
privacidad.s4.note: After these periods, data will be irreversibly deleted or anonymised.
privacidad.s5.title: 5. Recipients and processors
privacidad.s5.intro: Your data will not be transferred to third parties, except in the following cases:
privacidad.s5.processor1.name: Google LLC
privacidad.s5.processor1.desc: Web analytics service (Google Analytics) and fonts (Google Fonts). Google LLC, based in the US, acts as a processor under the EU-US Data Privacy Framework. See Google's privacy policy.
privacidad.s5.processor2.name: Hosting provider
privacidad.s5.processor2.desc: The web server hosting avson.eu may process connection data (IP, logs) in the context of providing hosting services, acting as a processor with appropriate contractual guarantees.
privacidad.s5.processor3.name: Legal obligation
privacidad.s5.processor3.desc: In case of legal requirement by the State Security Forces and Corps, judicial, tax or administrative authorities.
privacidad.s6.title: 6. International data transfers
privacidad.s6.p1: Some of our providers (in particular Google LLC) are based in the United States of America. These international transfers are carried out under the EU-US Data Privacy Framework, adopted by the European Commission by Adequacy Decision of 10 July 2023, which guarantees a level of protection equivalent to that of the European Economic Area.
privacidad.s6.p2: We do not carry out international transfers to countries that do not have an adequacy decision or appropriate safeguards under Art. 46 GDPR.
privacidad.s7.title: 7. Data subject rights
privacidad.s7.intro: In accordance with the GDPR and the LOPDGDD, you have the right to:
privacidad.s7.right1.title: Right of Access
privacidad.s7.right1.desc: Obtain confirmation as to whether we are processing your data and access to them, as well as information on the purposes, categories of data, recipients and retention periods.
privacidad.s7.right2.title: Right of Rectification
privacidad.s7.right2.desc: Request the correction of inaccurate or incomplete data concerning you.
privacidad.s7.right3.title: Right of Erasure ("right to be forgotten")
privacidad.s7.right3.desc: Request the deletion of your data when, among other reasons, they are no longer necessary for the purposes for which they were collected or you withdraw consent.
privacidad.s7.right4.title: Right of Portability
privacidad.s7.right4.desc: Receive your data in a structured, commonly used and machine-readable format and to transmit them to another controller when processing is based on consent or a contract.
privacidad.s7.right5.title: Right of Restriction
privacidad.s7.right5.desc: Request the suspension of the processing of your data in certain circumstances, such as while the accuracy of disputed data is verified.
privacidad.s7.right6.title: Right to Object
privacidad.s7.right6.desc: Object to the processing of your data on grounds relating to your particular situation, when processing is based on the legitimate interest of the controller.
privacidad.s7.right7.title: Right to withdraw consent
privacidad.s7.right7.desc: When processing is based on your consent, you may withdraw it at any time without affecting the lawfulness of processing based on consent before its withdrawal.
privacidad.s8.title: 8. How to exercise your rights
privacidad.s8.intro: You may exercise any of the above rights by sending a written request to:
privacidad.s8.postal: By post: Avson Consultoría Estratégica SLU — Calle Pedro Muñoz Seca 2 (Madrid) · Plaza Francesc Macià 7 (Barcelona)
privacidad.s8.subject: Subject: "GDPR rights exercise"
privacidad.s8.note: Your request must include: full name, copy of ID card or equivalent identification document, description of the right you wish to exercise and, where appropriate, supporting documentation. We will respond within a maximum of one month from receipt of the complete request, which may be extended by a further two months in cases of special complexity.
privacidad.s9.title: 9. Complaint to the supervisory authority
privacidad.s9.intro: If you consider that the processing of your personal data infringes applicable law, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the Spanish Data Protection Agency (AEPD), the competent supervisory authority in Spain:
privacidad.s9.aepd.name: Spanish Data Protection Agency (AEPD)
privacidad.s9.note: We recommend that before filing a complaint with the AEPD, you contact us to try to resolve the matter amicably.
privacidad.s10.title: 10. Cookies
privacidad.s10.text: This website uses its own and third-party cookies. For detailed information on the types of cookies used, their purposes, durations and how to manage them, please see our Cookie Policy.
privacidad.s11.title: 11. Changes to the Privacy Policy
privacidad.s11.p1: Avson Consultoría Estratégica SLU reserves the right to modify this privacy policy to adapt it to legislative, jurisprudential or business practice changes. Any material modification will be notified to users by a prominent notice on the website or, where appropriate, by email. The date of the last update is always shown at the top of this document.
privacidad.s11.p2: We recommend that you periodically review this page to stay informed about how we protect your information.

precio.stickyBar.text: 100% success guarantee in writing  ·  Request a no-obligation quote · We'll call you in <30 min (Mon–Fri 9am–6pm)  ·  Contact →
precio.hero.title: How much does ENS certification and ISO 27001 consulting cost in Spain.
precio.hero.intro: The price of GRC consulting depends on three factors: the size of your company, the category of the certificate and your starting point. Here we give you real ranges, no vagueness.
precio.factors.title: Factors that determine the price
precio.factors.subtitle: Three variables explain 90% of the price variation between similar projects.
precio.factors.card1.title: Size of the organisation
precio.factors.card1.text: The number of employees, systems in scope and physical locations determines the volume of documentation, controls to be implemented and audit time.
precio.factors.card2.title: Certificate category
precio.factors.card2.text: For ENS, the Basic, Medium or High category implies very different levels of requirement. The higher the category, the greater the number of mandatory controls and the more documentation required.
precio.factors.card3.title: Starting point
precio.factors.card3.text: An organisation that already has controls in place, up-to-date security documentation or a previous certification (ENS, ISO 9001, etc.) requires less work than one starting from scratch.
precio.table.title: Indicative price ranges
precio.table.subtitle: Prices in euros, excluding VAT. Full consulting until certificate is obtained.
precio.table.note: The final price is determined after the no-obligation quote, based on the scope, starting point and specific category of the project.
precio.arguments.title: Why Avson's price is worth it
precio.arguments.subtitle: We are not the cheapest on the market. We offer the best value for money.

arguments.items.title: Written success guarantee
arguments.items.text: If the certification body detects major non-conformities attributable to our consulting, we will bear the costs of the follow-up audit and remediation work at no charge. First audit success rate: 100%.

arguments.items.title: Speed: 3–4 months
arguments.items.text: The market standard takes 6 to 12 months. Our accelerated methodology takes medium-sized companies to audit in 90–120 days, reducing the time you don't have the certificate and uncertainty in tenders.

arguments.items.title: Cascade certifications
arguments.items.text: The ENS certificate covers 65% of ISO 27001 controls. If you certify in both with Avson, you save up to 40% compared to hiring two independent projects. One job, two certificates.

arguments.items.title: Lifelong relationship, not one-off
arguments.items.text: We include annual reviews, regulatory updates and preparation for follow-up audits in the full 3-year cycle. We don't disappear after the first certification.

precio.cta.title: Request a quote — personalised proposal within 48h.
precio.cta.desc: We analyse your current situation, tell you which certifications you need and provide a fixed quote. No surprises. No small print.
precio.faq.title: Frequently asked questions about pricing

faq.items.question: Does the price include the external audit?
faq.items.answer: No. The consulting fee covers all implementation work: gap analysis, documentation, training, control management and audit support. The fees of the accredited certification body (ENAC for ENS, or any accredited ISO certification body) are contracted directly with them and range between €2,000 and €6,000 depending on scope. In the no-obligation quote, we inform you of the exact certification cost for your specific case.

faq.items.question: Are there any additional costs not included?
faq.items.answer: The only costs not included in our proposal are the fees of the external certification body and any third-party technological tools you decide to implement. Our proposal is a fixed price: no surprises or additional budgets for minor scope changes. If the scope changes substantially during the project, we will agree with you before carrying out any additional work.

faq.items.question: Does the success guarantee have an additional cost?
faq.items.answer: No. The first-audit success guarantee is included in all our projects at no extra cost. If the certification body detects major non-conformities attributable to our consulting, we bear the costs of the follow-up audit and additional remediation work at no charge to the client. The guarantee is formalised in writing in the service contract.

faq.items.question: Can I finance the consulting?
faq.items.answer: Yes. We offer staggered payment plans in up to 6 interest-free monthly instalments for projects over €8,000. We also advise on available grants and subsidies — Kit Digital, Next Generation EU funds, INCIBE calls — that may cover part of the consulting cost depending on your company's profile. Ask us in the no-obligation quote and we will advise you on the options available for your case.

faq.items.question: How much do I save if I already have ENS and want ISO 27001?
faq.items.answer: The ENS certificate covers approximately 65% of ISO 27001 controls. If you are already certified in ENS Medium or High, the ISO 27001 certification project can be reduced by up to 50% compared to the standard price, since much of the documentation, risk analysis and controls are already implemented and audited. In the no-obligation quote, we give you an exact quote for the extension based on the current state of your management system.

iso22301.process.label: Our process
iso22301.process.title:
From zero to certified
in 5–6 months.
iso22301.process.subtitle: We start with your business, not templates: a plan that doesn't reflect your real operations won't protect you.

process.items.title: Business Impact Analysis (BIA)
process.items.description: We classify your processes by criticality and define RTO and RPO. The BIA determines what to protect, in what order and with what investment.

process.items.title: Continuity risk analysis
process.items.description: We assess threats that can stop your critical processes and define the continuity strategy for each.

process.items.title: BCP and DRP design and documentation
process.items.description: We draft the BCP (activation, crisis team, operational alternatives, communication) and the technical DRP to recover systems within the defined RTOs.

process.items.title: Testing and drills
process.items.description: We run real drills to validate RTOs, train the crisis team and fix gaps. This is where we differentiate ourselves: plans are truly tested.

process.items.title: Internal audit and certification
process.items.description: Internal audit and support through the two certification audit phases. Written guarantee: if you don't pass, we cover the next one at no cost.

iso22301.whatIs.title:
The international standard
for business continuity.
iso22301.whatIs.description: ISO 22301 certifies that your company can continue operating in the face of ransomware, system failure or disaster. It requires tested plans — not documents in a drawer. If you already have ENS or ISO 27001, we reuse up to 60% of the work.
iso22301.whatIs.concept1.title: BIA — Business Impact Analysis
iso22301.whatIs.concept1.text: Which processes are critical and how much each hour of downtime costs. The foundation of the entire BCMS.
iso22301.whatIs.concept2.title: BCP — Business Continuity Plan
iso22301.whatIs.concept2.text: How you continue operating during a crisis: activation, roles, alternatives and communication.
iso22301.whatIs.concept3.title: DRP — Disaster Recovery Plan
iso22301.whatIs.concept3.text: How you recover systems, infrastructure and data after the incident.
iso22301.whatIs.concept4.title: RTO and RPO
iso22301.whatIs.concept4.text: Maximum tolerable downtime and data loss: the numbers that define your resilience.

iso22301.faq.label: FAQ
iso22301.faq.title:
Everything about
ISO 22301.

faq.items.question: What exactly is the BCP and why is it so important?
faq.items.answer: It's the operational plan that describes how you keep functioning during a crisis: activation, crisis team, alternatives and communication. Without a tested BCP, there is no continuity.

faq.items.question: How long does the BIA take?
faq.items.answer: Between 2 and 4 weeks depending on complexity. It's the most important investment in the project: it defines the RTO and RPO for each process and how much you need to invest in continuity.

faq.items.question: How long does ISO 22301 certification take?
faq.items.answer: 5 to 6 months from start to certificate, compared to the market's 8–14. With prior ENS or ISO 27001, less.

faq.items.question: Which sectors have the greatest need for ISO 22301?
faq.items.answer: Finance, insurance, telecoms, healthcare, public administration, logistics, energy, cloud and industry with complex supply chains.

faq.items.question: What is the difference between a contingency plan and a certified BCMS?
faq.items.answer: A contingency plan is a document that is rarely tested. A certified BCMS requires periodic drills, management review and external validation that it actually works.

faq.items.question: Does DORA require ISO 22301?
faq.items.answer: Not literally, but it requires documented and tested continuity plans — exactly what ISO 22301 certifies. The overlap with DORA's continuity pillar is around 70%.

faq.items.question: How much does ISO 22301 certification cost?
faq.items.answer: Depends on size, critical processes and previous frameworks. With ENS or ISO 27001 the cost drops significantly. Request a quote: fixed price before signing.

faq.items.question: How long is the ISO 22301 certificate valid?
faq.items.answer: 3 years, with annual follow-ups and renewal in the third year. Our maintenance package manages audits and keeps plans up to date.

iso27001.faq.label: FAQ
iso27001.faq.title:
Everything you need
to know about ISO 27001.

faq.items.question: How much does ISO 27001 certification cost?
faq.items.answer: Depends on size, complexity and ISMS scope: for an SME it is much lower than commonly assumed. With ENS already certified, the 65% overlap reduces the cost significantly. Request a quote: fixed and detailed price before signing anything.

faq.items.question: How long does ISO 27001 certification take?
faq.items.answer: 4 to 5 months from start to certificate, compared to the market's 6–12. If you already have ENS, even less.

faq.items.question: Does ISO 27001:2022 require migration from the 2013 version?
faq.items.answer: Yes. The transition period ended in October 2025: if your 2013 certificate hasn't migrated, it is no longer valid. All our certifications are under the 2022 version.

faq.items.question: What is the difference between ISO 27001 and ENS?
faq.items.answer: ENS is mandatory for ICT providers to the Spanish Administration; ISO 27001 is the international standard required by the private market. They share 65% of controls — they are complementary.

faq.items.question: What is the ISMS scope?
faq.items.answer: It defines which processes, systems and services the certificate covers. Too broad increases cost; too narrow doesn't convince the client that demands it. We define the optimal scope with you.

faq.items.question: Do I need internal technical staff to implement ISO 27001?
faq.items.answer: No. Avson manages the entire project. You only need an internal point of contact and management commitment, which the standard requires.

faq.items.question: Does ISO 27001 help comply with NIS2 or DORA?
faq.items.answer: Yes: it covers 60–70% of NIS2 controls and DORA's ICT risk pillar. Certifying first in ISO 27001 reduces the cost of subsequent alignment.

faq.items.question: How long is the ISO 27001 certification valid?
faq.items.answer: 3 years, with annual follow-up audits and renewal in the third year. Our maintenance package manages them and keeps documentation up to date.

nis2.whatIs.title:
More scope, more requirements,
more penalties.
nis2.whatIs.description: NIS2 (Directive EU 2022/2555) makes cybersecurity mandatory in 18 sectors, with fines up to €10M and personal liability for directors. If you already have ENS or ISO 27001, 60–70% is covered: what's new is registration, notification to the regulator and supply chain.

nis2.sectors.important.sanction: Maximum fine: €7M or 1.4% of global revenue

sectors.important.items.text: Manufacturing and distribution of chemical products
Manufacturing (medical devices, computers, electronics, machinery, vehicles)
Digital service providers (marketplaces, search engines, social networks)

nis2.sectors.threshold: Size thresholds: applies from 50 employees or €10M revenue — and to certain critical entities regardless of size.
nis2.requirements.label: NIS2 obligations
nis2.requirements.title:
What NIS2 demands
from your organisation.
nis2.requirements.card1.title: Governance and risk management
nis2.requirements.card1.text: Management approves policies, is trained and responds personally: non-compliance can lead to disqualification.
nis2.requirements.card2.title: Incident management
nis2.requirements.card2.text: Notification to the supervisor within strict deadlines: alert within 24h, full notification within 72h, final report within 1 month.
nis2.requirements.card3.title: Business continuity
nis2.requirements.card3.text: Documented and tested continuity plans, with management involved in crisis drills. ISO 22301 covers it fully.
nis2.requirements.card4.title: Supply chain security
nis2.requirements.card4.text: Supplier due diligence and security clauses in contracts throughout the chain.
nis2.requirements.card5.title: Secure development
nis2.requirements.card5.text: Vulnerability management, secure development lifecycle (SDL) and periodic pentesting.
nis2.requirements.card6.title: Training and awareness
nis2.requirements.card6.text: Cybersecurity training for all staff and specific training for management — mandatory by directive.
nis2.synergies.label: Synergies with existing frameworks
nis2.synergies.title:
ENS + ISO 27001 cover
between 60% and 70% of NIS2.
nis2.synergies.intro: NIS2 makes law what ISO 27001 already implements. If you have any of these frameworks, we only close the gaps.
nis2.synergies.covered.heading: What ENS + ISO 27001 already cover (60-70%)
nis2.synergies.specific.heading: What is specific to NIS2 (30-40% additional)

synergies.specific.items.text: Registration as an affected entity with the regulator
Formal incident notification within 24/72h
Documented personal responsibility of directors
Formal assessment of supply chain security
Specific NIS2 training for management
Periodic review by the supervisory authority

nis2.faq.label: FAQ
nis2.faq.title:
Everything about
NIS2.

faq.items.question: Is NIS2 already in force in Spain?
faq.items.answer: The directive is in force in the EU and Spain is finalising its transposition. When the law is published, the compliance period will be short: being ahead is the winning position.

faq.items.question: What are the penalties under NIS2?
faq.items.answer: Essential: up to €10M or 2% of global revenue. Important: up to €7M or 1.4%. Additionally, licence suspension and temporary disqualification of directors.

faq.items.question: How do I know if I am an essential or important entity?
faq.items.answer: Depends on your sector and size (from 50 employees or €10M revenue). Request a scope analysis: we'll give you a definitive classification, for free.

faq.items.question: What is the difference between NIS2 and ISO 27001?
faq.items.answer: ISO 27001 is voluntary; NIS2 is law with penalties. ISO 27001 covers 60–70% of NIS2's technical requirements: it's the most efficient way to demonstrate compliance.

faq.items.question: Do DORA and NIS2 overlap?
faq.items.answer: Yes: for the financial sector DORA prevails — complying with DORA means you comply with NIS2. Outside finance they can coexist, with significant overlap.

faq.items.question: What responsibility does management have under NIS2?
faq.items.answer: Personal responsibility: directors approve measures, receive training and can be temporarily disqualified in case of serious non-compliance.

faq.items.question: How long does NIS2 alignment take?
faq.items.answer: With prior ENS or ISO 27001, 3–4 months. From scratch, 5–6 months. If urgent, we prioritise the highest regulatory risk gaps.

faq.items.question: Is there a formal NIS2 certification?
faq.items.answer: No. Compliance is demonstrated to the supervisor (INCIBE-CERT or CCN-CERT) with evidence and registration as an affected entity. ISO 27001 is the most recognised way to accredit the technical level.

otras.faq.label: FAQ
otras.faq.title:
ISO certifications:
your questions answered.

faq.items.question: Why ISO start if I don't have any certification?
faq.items.answer: Technology: ENS (public sector) or ISO 27001 (private). Industry or services: ISO 9001. With ESG commitments, add ISO 14001 at the same time. If in doubt, we'll resolve it in the free consultation.

faq.items.question: How much do I save with an integrated management system?
faq.items.answer: 25–35% in implementation and 30–40% in annual maintenance: one integrated audit instead of three.

faq.items.question: Can I certify ISO 9001 and ISO 14001 at the same time?
faq.items.answer: Yes, and it's common: they share structure, and certifying them together costs almost the same as one separately.

faq.items.question: How long does ISO 9001 certification take?
faq.items.answer: 3 to 4 months, all inclusive. With other ISO frameworks already implemented, 2–3 months.

faq.items.question: Is ISO 14001 mandatory?
faq.items.answer: Not legally; contractually, increasingly: large groups, tenders with sustainability criteria, green financing and CSRD reports effectively require it.

faq.items.question: What is the difference between HACCP and ISO 22000?
faq.items.answer: HACCP is legally mandatory in the EU; ISO 22000 integrates it into a certifiable management system with traceability, allergens and continuous improvement. It demonstrates that your HACCP works.

faq.items.question: Does ISO 55001 only apply to large companies?
faq.items.answer: No: it is scalable. It also brings value to medium-sized companies with industrial machinery, medical equipment or telecom infrastructure.

faq.items.question: Can I add ISO 9001 if I already have ENS certified?
faq.items.answer: Yes: they share 35% of documentation, and starting from ENS you can certify ISO 9001 in 2–3 months.

packs.faq.label: FAQ
packs.faq.title:
Everything about
the packs.

faq.items.question: How do certification packs work?
faq.items.answer: Packs combine multiple certifications into a single integrated project. The Avson team designs the project from the start to maximise framework overlap: risk analysis is done once for all, policies are written to meet each standard's requirements, and the internal audit covers all frameworks at once. The result is estimated savings of between 25% and 40% compared to independent projects, with significantly less client time commitment as well.

faq.items.question: What is included in the pack price?
faq.items.answer: All Avson packs include: initial gap analysis, full control implementation, complete management system documentation (policies, procedures, records), internal pre-certification audit, support during the certification audit with the certification body, and the success guarantee. What is not included in the pack price is the fee of the accredited certification body, which is paid directly to the certification body and depends on the size of the company and scope. Avson helps you select the certification body and negotiate the terms.

faq.items.question: Can I add a certification later if I start with just one?
faq.items.answer: Yes. If you start with ENS and later decide to add ISO 27001, the work already done for ENS significantly reduces the effort of the ISO 27001 project. The savings are not as large as if you had started with the full pack from the beginning (because in the pack we design documentation to be reused from the outset), but it is still greater than 20% compared to a completely new ISO 27001 project. Many of our clients start with one certification and add more as their company grows.

faq.items.question: What if I already have a certification from another consultancy?
faq.items.answer: We can take over and add the next framework to the one you already have. We start with a review of the existing management system to understand what is implemented, what works well and what can be improved. From there, we design the extension project leveraging the work already done. It is not as efficient as having started the pack from scratch with Avson, but we are still able to extract significant overlap from the previous work.

faq.items.question: Is the pack timeline the total time or do the timings run in parallel?
faq.items.answer: The pack timeline is the total duration of the integrated project, not the sum of individual timelines. In most packs, frameworks are implemented sequentially or with partial overlap, depending on dependencies between them. Risk analysis and base documentation are done at the beginning and serve all frameworks. Specific phases for each standard are developed once that base is established. The result is that the Triple Pack (ENS + ISO 27001 + ISO 22301) takes 7-9 months, compared to the 12-15 months that three separate projects would take.

faq.items.question: Are there flexible payment options?
faq.items.answer: Yes. We work with staggered payments aligned to the project's rhythm. Typically: a percentage at project start, another percentage at the midpoint, and the remainder upon obtaining the certificate. This payment structure aligns our interests with the client's: most of the payment is unlocked when the goal is achieved. For projects with annual maintenance packages, the package is paid at the start of each maintenance year.

faq.items.question: What exactly does the success guarantee include?
faq.items.answer: Avson's success guarantee means that if, following our methodology and the agreed work plan, we do not pass the certification audit on the first attempt, Avson covers the cost of the second audit with the certification body at no additional cost to the client. This guarantee applies when the client has followed Avson's team recommendations and completed the project phases as planned. The guarantee is included in the contract of all certification projects, not an optional add-on.

faq.items.question: Can I start without being sure which pack I need?
faq.items.answer: Yes. Avson's no-obligation quote is precisely designed for that: to analyse your situation, your clients, your regulatory requirements and your business objectives, and recommend the most efficient pack for your specific case. Many clients arrive not knowing exactly what they need — they know they need 'some certifications' but not which ones or in what order. The first meeting with the consultant resolves that doubt in 45 minutes.

faq.items.question: When should I hire to have the certificate before a specific tender?
faq.items.answer: For a standard process of 3–4 months (ENS Basic or Medium), you need to hire at least 4 months before the date you need to present the certificate — and a bit more if you want a safety margin. If you have a tender closing sooner, there is the ENS Express service (30 calendar days), but slots are very limited: only 6 Express projects per month. Check availability as soon as possible — the more days we have, the better we guarantee the result and the more comfortable the implementation is for your team.

equipo.howWeWork.label: Our way of working
equipo.howWeWork.title:
15 specialised professionals.
The GRC team that guarantees your certification.
equipo.howWeWork.body: Avson is a specialised team, not a general-purpose consultancy. That specialisation has a price — we don't do everything — but it also has a clear advantage: when you hire us for a GRC project, you are working with the people who have done the most projects of that type in Spain.
equipo.howWeWork.card1.title: One point of contact per project
equipo.howWeWork.card1.text: Each client has an assigned consultant who knows the project from start to finish. We don't change teams halfway through. You don't have to repeat the context every time you call. The consultant who starts the project is the one who finishes it. That continuity shows in the quality of the result and the speed of execution.
equipo.howWeWork.card2.title: Full transparency from day one
equipo.howWeWork.card2.text: We know what you're asking for from day one and we tell you if it's achievable within your timeline and budget. If something isn't possible, we tell you before you sign, not three months later when you've already paid half. Honesty about what is feasible is the principle of every project that ends well.
equipo.howWeWork.card3.title: Written guarantee on every project
equipo.howWeWork.card3.text: We don't sign any certification contract without including the audit success guarantee. If following our methodology we don't pass the first audit, we cover the second at no additional cost. This is our way of aligning interests: if you don't get certified, we don't collect the full project fee. It couldn't be clearer.
equipo.howWeWork.card4.title: Long-term relationship
equipo.howWeWork.card4.text: The goal is not to deliver the certificate and disappear. The goal is to be your reference GRC team as your company grows and adds regulatory frameworks. Most of our clients repeat with us: the second project is faster, cheaper and better than the first because we already know each other.
equipo.creds.label: Credentials
equipo.creds.title:
The certifications
that validate the team.
equipo.creds.body: A GRC consultant must know the regulations they implement from the inside. Our team maintains active professional certifications that guarantee technical knowledge is always up to date.

The only GRC consultancy

ens.comparison.title:
Declaration of conformity vs
ENS certificate: they are not the same.
ens.comparison.intro: Two ways to demonstrate ENS — and many companies don't know the difference until they've already lost a tender.
ens.comparison.declaration.title: Declaration of Conformity
ens.comparison.declaration.text: It is a self-declaration by the supplier. The company declares that it complies with the ENS requirements without an external entity verifying it. It is only valid for the Basic category and carries less weight in tenders.
ens.comparison.declaration.list:
• Only valid for ENS Basic
  • No external audit required
  • Lower credibility with contracting bodies
  • Many tenders explicitly reject it
ens.comparison.certificate.title: ENS Certificate
ens.comparison.certificate.text: Issued by a certification body accredited by ENAC after an independent audit. It is the standard required by most relevant tenders and the only one that provides full legal certainty.
ens.comparison.certificate.list:
• Valid for Basic, Medium and High
  • Verified by an accredited external body
  • Accepted in all tenders
  • Only one recognised in high-criticality contracts
ens.comparison.footer: Avson manages the full ENS certification process with ENAC-accredited bodies: Bureau Veritas, SGS, AENOR, Applus+ and BSI.
ens.issuer.title:
Who issues the ENS certificate
and how is it chosen?
ens.issuer.entities: Bureau Veritas · AENOR · SGS · Applus+ · BSI Group
ens.issuer.text: We work with all ENAC-accredited bodies and reserve your audit slot.

ens.faq.label: FAQ
ens.faq.title:
Everything you need
to know about ENS.

faq.items.question: What is the National Security Scheme (ENS)?
faq.items.answer: It is the legal framework (Royal Decree 311/2022) that sets out the security requirements for information systems of the Spanish public sector. It is mandatory for public administrations and for private companies that provide services to them.

faq.items.question: How much does ENS certification cost?
faq.items.answer: Depends on the category (Basic, Medium or High) and the scope of your systems. We give you a fixed price before starting, with the written guarantee included. See indicative prices.

faq.items.question: How long does the ENS certificate take?
faq.items.answer: Between 9 and 14 weeks for a standard project. If your tender closes sooner, ENS Express delivers in 30 days with a dedicated team.

faq.items.question: Who issues the certificate? Will any consultancy do?
faq.items.answer: The certificate is issued by an ENAC-accredited body (AENOR, Bureau Veritas, SGS, Applus+, BSI) after an independent audit. The consultancy — us — prepares your company to pass it. We also coordinate the audit booking.

faq.items.question: Does it also work for municipalities and universities?
faq.items.answer: Yes. We implement ENS both for supplier companies and for public administrations: municipalities, provincial councils, universities and public entities that must comply by law.

faq.items.question: If I already have ISO 27001, how much work is left for ENS?
faq.items.answer: About 65% of the work is already done: we leverage your documentation and only cover the differences. The timeline and cost drop significantly.

ens-medio.hero.label: National Security Scheme · Medium Category · RD 311/2022
ens-medio.hero.title:
ENS Medium certificate: the category required by
most public ICT contracts.
ens-medio.hero.description: The ENS Medium category is the de facto standard of the public ICT procurement market in Spain. If you want to bid for contracts with the General State Administration, ministries or Autonomous Communities, you need ENS Medium. Avson will certify you in 3-4 months nationwide.
ens-medio.whatIs.title:
ENS Medium category:
when a failure causes serious harm.
ens-medio.whatIs.description: The Medium category applies when an incident would cause serious harm to the organisation, citizens or the public service: for example, systems that manage citizen data at a regional scale or services whose interruption affects public provision. It is the category required by most ICT tenders of the General State Administration and the Autonomous Communities.
ens-medio.whatIs.description2: Unlike Basic, it does not allow a Declaration of Conformity: it always requires an external audit by an ENAC-accredited body.
ens-medio.whatIs.legalBox1.label: Legal framework
ens-medio.whatIs.legalBox1.text: Royal Decree 311/2022, of 3 May. Articles 40-43 and Annex I (categorisation) and Annex II (security measures for Medium level).
ens-medio.whatIs.legalBox2.label: Impact determining Medium category
ens-medio.whatIs.legalBox2.text: The maximum potential impact in any of the five dimensions reaches the "Medium" level: serious harm to the organisation, citizens or public service.
ens-medio.whatIs.legalBox3.label: Audit always mandatory
ens-medio.whatIs.legalBox3.text: ENS Medium does not allow a Declaration of Conformity. It always requires an external audit by an ENAC-accredited body.

ens-medio.controls.title:
ENS Medium controls:
greater requirement, greater protection.
ens-medio.controls.description: ENS Medium requires about 60 security measures from Annex II of RD 311/2022, compared to 45 for Basic and with higher requirements for each: mandatory two-factor authentication, continuous monitoring, formal vulnerability management, encryption in transit and at rest, and tested continuity plans with defined RTO/RPO.
ens-medio.controls.description2: At Avson we implement all these controls with a proven methodology and AI tools, reducing timelines without compromising quality.
ens-medio.controls.item1.title: Two-factor authentication (2FA)
ens-medio.controls.item1.text: Mandatory for access to systems in scope. No exception in Medium category.
ens-medio.controls.item2.title: Continuous monitoring
ens-medio.controls.item2.text: Logs with greater detail and retention. Documented periodic analysis of security events.
ens-medio.controls.item3.title: Formal vulnerability management
ens-medio.controls.item3.text: Periodic and documented vulnerability analysis. Mandatory remediation plan.
ens-medio.controls.item4.title: Tested continuity plans
ens-medio.controls.item4.text: Defined RTO/RPO. Documented periodic recovery tests with evidence.
ens-medio.controls.item5.text: 15 additional controls compared to ENS Basic, with greater detail and evidence.
ens-medio.standard.title:
Why ENS Medium is the de facto standard
of public ICT contracting.
ens-medio.standard.description: Most relevant ICT tenders require ENS Medium as a minimum, even if the systems could technically be classified as Basic: for the public buyer it is a solvency filter. With only ENS Basic you may be losing contracts that technically you could win. That's why we recommend choosing the category based on the market you want to access, not just on your current systems.
ens-medio.standard.compare1.title: ENS Basic gives you access to...
ens-medio.standard.compare1.text: Tenders from small municipalities, provincial councils and local bodies that specify the Basic category.
ens-medio.standard.compare2.title: ENS Medium gives you access to...
ens-medio.standard.compare2.text: All of the above plus: General State Administration, ministries, Autonomous Communities, autonomous bodies, public companies, universities and bodies of any size across Spain.
ens-medio.standard.compare3.title: ENS Medium includes Basic
ens-medio.standard.compare3.text: An ENS Medium certificate demonstrates compliance with Basic category requirements. You don't need both certificates.

ens-medio.iso.description: 65% of ENS Medium controls overlap with ISO/IEC 27001:2022. If you already have ISO 27001, ENS Medium is much faster and cheaper: we reuse your ISMS and documentation. If you start with ENS Medium, two-thirds of the path to ISO 27001 is already done — that's why the ENS + ISO 27001 pack is Avson's most demanded.

ens-medio.faq.title:
Frequently asked questions about
the ENS Medium certificate.

faq.items.question: How do I know if my company needs ENS Basic or Medium?
faq.items.answer: The category is determined by the potential impact of a security incident on systems providing services to the Public Administration. If the highest possible impact in any of the five dimensions — confidentiality, integrity, availability, authenticity or traceability — is "Medium", then the category is Medium. In practice, you also need to analyse which contracts you want to win: if the target tenders require ENS Medium, you should certify at Medium even if your systems could technically be Basic. Avson performs this analysis for free as part of the initial diagnosis.

faq.items.question: Does ENS Medium also work for contracts requiring ENS Basic?
faq.items.answer: Yes. ENS Medium is a higher level than Basic, so an ENS Medium certificate automatically demonstrates compliance with Basic category requirements. If you have ENS Medium, you can bid for any contract requiring ENS Basic or ENS Medium. You don't need both certificates separately. This makes ENS Medium particularly cost-effective: one certification covers the widest range of tender opportunities.

faq.items.question: How much more demanding is ENS Medium compared to Basic?
faq.items.answer: ENS Medium requires approximately 60 measures compared to 45 for Basic, and with greater detail and evidence for each. The most relevant differences are: two-factor authentication (2FA) is mandatory for access to systems in scope; security monitoring must be continuous and documented; formal vulnerability management with periodic analysis is required; service continuity plans must be documented, tested and up to date; and incident management requires formal notification and post-incident analysis procedures. ENS Medium does not allow a Declaration of Conformity: it always requires an external ENAC audit.

faq.items.question: Do I have to renew the ENS Medium periodically?
faq.items.answer: Yes. The ENS Medium certificate is valid for two years from the date of issue. Before expiry, it is mandatory to undergo a new renewal audit to maintain the certification. Without a valid ENS certificate, you are again excluded from tenders that require it. At Avson we proactively manage this process: we notify you six months in advance and coordinate the entire renewal process as part of our ongoing relationship with each client.

faq.items.question: If I already have ISO 27001, how long does ENS Medium take?
faq.items.answer: If you already have an active and mature ISO 27001 certified ISMS, the ENS Medium process can be significantly reduced. 65% of controls overlap, meaning much of the documentation, procedures and technical controls already exist. At Avson we perform a specific gap analysis for clients with ISO 27001 and design a plan that reuses existing work as much as possible. Total time can be reduced to 6-8 weeks in the best cases, compared to the standard 3-4 months for companies without a prior base.

ens-express.faq.label: FAQ
ens-express.faq.title:
Everything you need to know
about ENS Express.

faq.items.question: Does ENS Express guarantee ENS Basic, Medium or High?
faq.items.answer: ENS Express covers ENS Basic and ENS Medium. ENS High requires a deeper security analysis that cannot be completed in 30 days without compromising quality. If you need urgent ENS High, consult us — we evaluate each case individually.

faq.items.question: What happens if we don't get the certificate in 30 days?
faq.items.answer: The success guarantee also applies to Express. If we follow our methodology and the agreed work plan and we do not pass the audit, we repeat the process at no additional cost. That is our written guarantee, not a marketing promise.

faq.items.question: What do I need to prepare before starting?
faq.items.answer: Very little. We need access to the systems in scope, an owner with decision-making authority, and availability for daily 30-minute meetings. We manage everything else: documentation, technical implementation, coordination with the audit body and response to non-conformities.

faq.items.question: Does it work for any sector?
faq.items.answer: Yes. We have completed Express projects in the ICT, defence, healthcare and local government sectors. The appropriate ENS category is determined in the initial analysis within the first 48 hours, before intensive work begins.

faq.items.question: How much does ENS Express cost?
faq.items.answer: The price varies depending on the complexity and starting point of your organisation. The initial diagnosis is free and we give you the budget on the same call. As a reference, Express has a 50–70% premium over the standard service, justified by the dedicated team and project intensity. When compared to the value of the public contract you risk losing, the return is immediate.

ens-basico.hero.label: National Security Scheme · Basic Category · RD 311/2022
ens-basico.hero.title:
ENS Basic certificate: what it is, who needs it
and how to obtain it.
ens-basico.hero.description: The Basic category of ENS is the gateway to the public procurement market. At Avson GRC we support you through the entire process, from diagnosis to certificate issuance, nationwide, in 3-4 months.
ens-basico.whatIs.title:
ENS Basic category:
for systems with limited impact.
ens-basico.whatIs.description: The Basic category applies when an incident would have limited impact: if in the five security dimensions (confidentiality, integrity, availability, authenticity, traceability) the maximum impact is "Low", your system is Basic. It is the usual category for suppliers to municipalities and less critical bodies.
ens-basico.whatIs.description2: The category is determined by the system in scope, not the company. At Avson we analyse your scope for free and without obligation.
ens-basico.whatIs.legalBox1.label: Legal framework
ens-basico.whatIs.legalBox1.text: Royal Decree 311/2022, of 3 May. Articles 40-43 and Annex I (categorisation) and Annex II (security measures).
ens-basico.whatIs.legalBox2.label: Security dimensions
ens-basico.whatIs.legalBox2.text: Confidentiality · Integrity · Availability · Authenticity · Traceability. The highest level in any of them determines the category of the system.
ens-basico.whatIs.legalBox3.label: Impact in Basic category
ens-basico.whatIs.legalBox3.text: The maximum potential impact of an incident in any of the dimensions does not exceed the "Low" level according to the RD 311/2022 scale.
ens-basico.whoNeeds.title: Who needs the ENS Basic certificate?
ens-basico.whoNeeds.subtitle: The profiles that most often certify in the Basic category:

whoNeeds.items.title: Municipal management software
whoNeeds.items.text: Registry, licence, local tax applications or transparency portals for municipalities.

whoNeeds.items.title: Communications for local public administrations
whoNeeds.items.text: Connectivity, IP PBX, videoconferencing or network management for commonwealths and local bodies.

whoNeeds.items.title: Cloud for local bodies
whoNeeds.items.text: Cloud services (IaaS, SaaS, PaaS) for local entities with administrative support systems.

whoNeeds.items.title: ICT consultancies for provincial councils
whoNeeds.items.text: Support, development or maintenance of systems for provincial councils, consortia and supra-municipal entities.

whoNeeds.items.title: Document management and digital signature
whoNeeds.items.text: File management, electronic archiving or digital signature for smaller-scale administrations.

whoNeeds.items.title: EdTech and educational platforms
whoNeeds.items.text: LMS platforms, school management or technology solutions for public education centres.

ens-basico.whoNeeds.ctaQuote: "Don't know if you need ENS Basic or Medium? Avson's no-obligation quote will tell you within 24h."
ens-basico.controls.title:
ENS Basic controls:
what you need to implement.
ens-basico.controls.description: ENS Basic requires about 45 security measures from Annex II of RD 311/2022, divided into three frameworks: organisational, operational and protective. The requirement is lower than in Medium: a strong password is sufficient (no two-factor) and monitoring and logs are lighter.
ens-basico.controls.description2: At Avson we accelerate documentation and MAGERIT risk analysis with AI tools, reducing timelines without losing rigour before the audit.
ens-basico.controls.item1.title: Organisational framework (org)
ens-basico.controls.item1.text: Security policy, usage rules, procedures and defined security roles.
ens-basico.controls.item2.title: Operational framework (op)
ens-basico.controls.item2.text: Planning, access control, configuration management, continuity and incident management.
ens-basico.controls.item3.title: Protection measures (mp)
ens-basico.controls.item3.text: Facilities, personnel, equipment, communications, media, applications and services.
ens-basico.controls.item4.text: ~45 applicable measures. Subset of the total 73 Annex II controls. Requirement level lower than Medium and High.
ens-basico.declaration.title:
Declaration of Conformity or ENS Basic certificate?
The difference matters.
ens-basico.declaration.description: Only in the Basic category can you demonstrate compliance with a Declaration of Conformity: a self-declaration without an external ENAC audit. It seems cheaper and faster, but has important limitations:
ens-basico.declaration.check1: Most public procurement specifications explicitly require the ENS certificate issued by an ENAC-accredited audit body, even if the system is Basic. In those cases, the Declaration of Conformity is not accepted.
ens-basico.declaration.check2: The Declaration of Conformity has lower legal and market value: it does not demonstrate the intervention of an independent third party validating effective compliance with controls.
ens-basico.declaration.check3: In competitive tenders, having the ENS certificate versus a mere declaration can give you an advantage in the technical scoring of assessable criteria.
ens-basico.declaration.check4: If in the future you expand your certification scope or your systems increase in category, you will have to transition to the certificate anyway. It's better to start from the correct base.
ens-basico.declaration.conclusion: We analyse your tender before recommending. General rule: go directly for the certificate — it costs little more and opens up much more market.
ens-basico.declaration.boxDeclaration.title: Declaration of Conformity
ens-basico.declaration.boxDeclaration.con1: Not accepted in most relevant tenders
ens-basico.declaration.boxCertificate.title: ENS Basic Certificate
ens-basico.declaration.boxCertificate.pro1: Accepted in all ENS Basic tenders
ens-basico.declaration.boxCertificate.pro5: Basis for ENS Medium, ISO 27001 and NIS2

How we get your ENS Basic certificate
in 3-4 months with a guarantee.
ens-basico.process.subtitle: Proven methodology with 355+ certified companies, nationwide, on-site or remote, always with a written success guarantee.

process.items.title: Diagnosis and scope analysis
process.items.text: Systems in scope, existing controls and applicable ENS category. Gap analysis and personalised work plan, free and without obligation.

process.items.title: Control implementation
process.items.text: The ~45 ENS Basic measures: ISMS documentation, MAGERIT risk analysis, technical controls and evidence. Specialised AI to accelerate timelines.

process.items.title: Internal audit and pre-audit
process.items.text: Full internal audit to correct non-conformities before the external one, and preparation for each session with the ENAC body.

process.items.title: ENAC certification audit
process.items.text: We coordinate everything with the ENAC body and respond to their reports. 100% certification rate, with written guarantee.

ens-basico.auditors.title: Who can audit ENS Basic?
ens-basico.auditors.description: Only a certification body accredited by ENAC gives legal validity to the certificate: Bureau Veritas, SGS, AENOR, Applus+ or BSI, among others, each with its own timelines and fees. At Avson we select the most suitable for your profile, coordinate the sessions and respond to reports until the certificate is issued. You don't have to manage anything with the auditor.

ens-basico.auditors.entity1.desc: ENAC accredited · ENS Basic, Medium and High

ens-basico.auditors.entity4.name: Applus+ / BSI

ens-basico.cost.title:
How much does the ENS Basic certificate cost?
Less than what you lose without it.
ens-basico.cost.intro: Cost depends on size, systems in scope and your starting security maturity. General guidance:
ens-basico.cost.tierS.text: Reduced scope, agile process. The external audit is the main cost component.
ens-basico.cost.tierM.text: The most common range. The investment pays for itself with the first public contract after certification.
ens-basico.cost.tierL.text: Broader scope. With measures already implemented, cost decreases; there are synergies with ISO 27001 in parallel.
ens-basico.cost.urgency: Every week without the ENS Basic certificate is a tender you cannot apply for. Request a quote and receive a personalised proposal within 48 hours.
ens-basico.faq.title:
Frequently asked questions about
the ENS Basic certificate.

faq.items.question: Is the Declaration of Conformity sufficient or do I need the ENS Basic certificate?
faq.items.answer: It depends on the specific tender you want to apply for. RD 311/2022 allows the Declaration of Conformity for the Basic category as an alternative to the formal certificate, but the vast majority of public bodies explicitly require the certificate issued by an ENAC-accredited body, especially for contracts of a certain size or with a competitive component. At Avson we always analyse the specific tender before recommending, although as a general rule we recommend going directly for the certificate: the cost difference is small and the market value difference is huge.

faq.items.question: Does the ENS Basic expire?
faq.items.answer: Yes. The ENS Basic certificate is valid for two years from its issuance. After that period, it is necessary to undergo a new renewal audit to maintain the certification. At Avson we proactively monitor the expiry of your certificate and notify you six months in advance to start the renewal process with plenty of time. The Declaration of Conformity, on the other hand, does not have a strictly defined expiry date, but must be reviewed when systems or the risk context change.

faq.items.question: How many controls does ENS Basic have?
faq.items.answer: ENS Basic requires the implementation of approximately 45 security measures from Annex II of RD 311/2022. They are a subset of the total of 73 controls, applied with a lower level of requirement than the Medium (approximately 60 measures) and High (all 73 measures at maximum level) categories. The exact selection of applicable controls in each case depends on the risk analysis, defined scope and specific characteristics of the organisation's systems.

faq.items.question: What is the difference between ENS Basic and ENS Medium?
faq.items.answer: The fundamental difference is the level of impact a security incident could cause to the organisation, citizens or the public service. Basic applies when impact would be limited; Medium when it could be serious. In terms of controls, ENS Medium requires additional measures not mandatory in Basic: two-factor authentication (2FA), continuous security monitoring, formal and documented incident management, higher service continuity requirements and more detailed recovery plans. Additionally, ENS Medium does not allow a Declaration of Conformity: it always requires an external audit by an ENAC-accredited body.

faq.items.question: Is ENS Basic valid for any public tender?
faq.items.answer: No. ENS Basic is only valid for tenders that explicitly specify that category or do not require a higher category. Many relevant contracts of the General State Administration, ministries and Autonomous Communities require ENS Medium as a minimum, regardless of contract size. That is why it is essential to review the specific tender before starting the certification process. Avson's no-obligation quote always includes this tender analysis and the recommendation of the appropriate category.

ens-alto.hero.label: National Security Scheme · High Category · RD 311/2022
ens-alto.hero.title:
ENS High certificate: for critical systems
where failure is not an option.
ens-alto.hero.description: The High category of ENS represents the highest level of requirement of the National Security Scheme. For systems whose failure could compromise national security, mass citizen data or essential infrastructure. Avson offers specialised ENS High consulting nationwide.
ens-alto.whatIs.title:
ENS High category:
when an incident has very serious consequences.
ens-alto.whatIs.description: The High category applies when a failure would have very serious or irreversible consequences: national security, critical infrastructure, mass citizen data or essential services. It requires the 73 controls of Annex II of RD 311/2022 at their maximum level: reinforced physical security, advanced cryptography, real-time monitoring and the strictest continuity of the scheme.
ens-alto.whatIs.description2: Avson provides consultants specialised in high-security environments, with mastery of CCN-STIC guides and experience in auditing critical systems.
ens-alto.whatIs.legalBox1.label: Legal framework
ens-alto.whatIs.legalBox1.text: Royal Decree 311/2022, of 3 May. Articles 40-43, Annex I (High level categorisation) and Annex II (73 measures at maximum level).
ens-alto.whatIs.legalBox2.label: Impact determining High category
ens-alto.whatIs.legalBox2.text: Very serious or irreversible consequences: compromise of national security, interruption of essential services, massive exposure of citizen data.
ens-alto.whatIs.legalBox3.label: Controls required
ens-alto.whatIs.legalBox3.text: All 73 controls of Annex II of RD 311/2022 at their maximum application level. Includes stricter physical, cryptographic and availability requirements.
ens-alto.whoNeeds.title: Who needs the ENS High certificate?
ens-alto.whoNeeds.subtitle: The typical profiles in the High category:

whoNeeds.items.title: Defence and national security

ens-alto.controls.title:
The 73 controls of ENS High:
the highest level of requirement.
ens-alto.controls.description: ENS High requires the 73 controls of Annex II at maximum level: physical security with video surveillance and redundancy, cryptography with CCN-certified algorithms and devices, mandatory encryption in transit and at rest, and monitoring with real-time detection. Continuity adds semi-annual tests, very tight RTO/RPO and redundancy of critical systems.
ens-alto.controls.item1.title: CCN-certified advanced cryptography
ens-alto.controls.item1.text: Certified key lengths and algorithms. Homologated encryption devices for critical systems.

ens-alto.controls.item3.title: Reinforced physical security
ens-alto.controls.item3.text: Strict physical access control, video surveillance and physical redundancy of critical facilities.
ens-alto.controls.item4.title: High-availability continuity
ens-alto.controls.item4.text: Very tight RTO/RPO. Semi-annual recovery tests. Generator with 2h autonomy.
ens-alto.controls.item5.text: All 73 controls of Annex II applied at their highest level of requirement.
ens-alto.ccn.title:
ENS High and the National Cryptologic Centre:
the CCN's role in supervision.
ens-alto.ccn.description: The CCN technically supervises ENS and develops the CCN-STIC guides; for systems with classified information it may impose additional requirements and designate authorised auditors. ENS High operators must also report incidents to the CCN-CERT according to CCN-STIC 817 guides. Our consultants master these guides and have direct experience liaising with the CCN.

ens-alto.ccn.guide1.text: ENS implementation guide. Main reference for the implementation process.
ens-alto.ccn.guide2.title: CCN-STIC 808
ens-alto.ccn.guide2.text: Verification of compliance with ENS security measures.
ens-alto.ccn.guide3.title: CCN-STIC 817
ens-alto.ccn.guide3.text: Cyber incident management. Notification to CCN-CERT for critical systems.
ens-alto.ccn.guide4.title: CCN-STIC 500 and 600 series
ens-alto.ccn.guide4.text: Technical guides by technology: servers, applications, communications, operating systems.

At Avson it is led by senior consultants with proven experience in high-security environments, with their own approach and team, not an adaptation of ENS Basic or Medium.

ens-alto.faq.title:
Frequently asked questions about
the ENS High certificate.

faq.items.question: How is it determined if my systems are High category?
faq.items.answer: The High category is determined when the potential impact of a security incident in any of the five dimensions — confidentiality, integrity, availability, authenticity or traceability — reaches the "High" level according to the RD 311/2022 assessment scale. In concrete terms, this occurs when an incident could have very serious or irreversible consequences: compromising national security, disabling critical infrastructure, massively exposing citizen data or interrupting essential services. The formal analysis must be carried out using the MAGERIT methodology and documented in the risk analysis. Avson can provide an initial indicative assessment for free.

faq.items.question: Is ENS High more expensive than Medium?
faq.items.answer: Yes, significantly. ENS High requires the implementation of all 73 controls of Annex II at their maximum level, which requires a greater investment in specialised consulting, technical controls (SIEM, advanced cryptography, physical redundancy) and a more extensive and intensive external audit. The timeline is also longer: between 5 and 7 months depending on project complexity. However, ENS High opens access to very high-value contracts that more than justify the investment.

faq.items.question: Which entities can audit ENS High?
faq.items.answer: The ENS High audit must be carried out by a certification body accredited by ENAC for the ENS scheme: Bureau Veritas, SGS, AENOR, Applus+, BSI and others with valid accreditation. For systems handling classified information or related to defence and national security, auditors authorised by the CCN (National Cryptologic Centre) may also be involved, with specific competencies for these environments. Avson coordinates the selection of the most suitable audit body for each ENS High project.

faq.items.question: Is ENS High equivalent to ISO 27001?
faq.items.answer: They are not equivalent although they share a significant base of controls (approximately 65%). ENS High has specific requirements of the Spanish Public Administration context and stricter controls in areas such as service continuity, CCN-certified cryptography and physical security. ISO 27001 has international scope, is more flexible in its application and is oriented to the private sector. Both certifications complement each other very well: ENS High together with ISO 27001 provides the highest possible coverage for public and private clients.

faq.items.question: Do my High category systems also need ENS Basic or Medium for other systems?
faq.items.answer: It is perfectly possible for the same organisation to have systems of different ENS categories. Categorisation is done system by system, based on the potential impact of each. Support, administrative or secondary systems can be Basic or Medium category even if the main systems are High. This situation is common in large organisations: they maintain an ENS High scope for their critical systems and an ENS Medium or Basic scope for the rest. Avson helps correctly define the scope of each certification to optimise effort and costs.

dora.whatIs.title:
The European regulation that makes
digital resilience mandatory.
dora.whatIs.description: DORA (Regulation EU 2022/2554) requires financial entities and their ICT providers to demonstrate digital operational resilience. In force since January 2025, with no grace period. It is structured into five pillars — and we cover them all.
dora.pillars.p1.title: ICT risk management
dora.pillars.p1.text: Documented technology risk framework, approved by the governing body, which is personally accountable for it.
dora.pillars.p2.title: ICT incident management and notification
dora.pillars.p2.text: Strict notification deadlines to the regulator: alert within 4 hours, full notification within 24 hours, final report within 1 month.
dora.pillars.p3.title: Digital operational resilience testing
dora.pillars.p3.text: Periodic vulnerability and continuity tests; TLPT (simulated attacks by certified providers) for systemic entities.
dora.pillars.p4.title: ICT third-party risk
dora.pillars.p4.text: Up-to-date register of ICT providers and contracts with mandatory security, audit and continuity clauses.
dora.pillars.p5.title: Threat information sharing
dora.pillars.p5.text: Participation in sectoral threat intelligence platforms: voluntary, but valued by the supervisor.

dora.faq.label: FAQ
dora.faq.title:
Everything you need
to know about DORA.

faq.items.question: Who exactly does the DORA Regulation apply to?
faq.items.answer: The 21 types of financial entities in Article 2 (banks, payment, investment, insurance, crypto-assets…) and their ICT providers, whether by designation of regulators or by contractual requirement of their financial clients.

faq.items.question: What are the penalties for DORA non-compliance?
faq.items.answer: In Spain, the Bank of Spain, CNMV and DGSFP impose sanctions. For critical ICT providers, the ESAs can fine up to 1% of global daily turnover or ban service provision in the EU.

faq.items.question: When did DORA come into force?
faq.items.answer: On 17 January 2025, directly applicable throughout the EU without transposition. If it applies to you, you should already be aligned.

faq.items.question: How do DORA and NIS2 relate?
faq.items.answer: For the financial sector, DORA prevails over NIS2: complying with DORA means you comply with NIS2. No need to duplicate frameworks.

faq.items.question: What is TLPT (Threat-Led Penetration Testing)?
faq.items.answer: An advanced penetration test based on real threats, required only for systemic entities. For most, vulnerability analyses, standard pentesting and continuity exercises suffice.

faq.items.question: What contractual clauses does DORA require with ICT providers?
faq.items.answer: Description of services, SLAs, data location, incident management, audit rights, exit plans and subcontracting. We review and adapt your contracts as part of the project.

faq.items.question: How long does the DORA alignment process take?
faq.items.answer: With ENS or ISO 27001 already implemented, 3–4 months. From scratch, 5–6 months. If regulatory urgency, we accelerate.

faq.items.question: Is there a formal DORA certification?
faq.items.answer: No. Compliance is demonstrated to the supervisor with evidence: resilience tests, incident logs and reviewed contracts. It is continuous, not a one-off certificate.

cookies.hero.label: Legal
cookies.hero.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.s1.title: 1. What are cookies?
cookies.s1.p1: Cookies are small text files that websites install on the user's device (computer, smartphone, tablet) when they visit them. They allow the website to remember information about the visit — such as preferred language and other options — to facilitate the next visit and make the site more useful to you.
cookies.s1.p2: This cookie policy is governed by article 22.2 of Law 34/2002, of 11 July, on Information Society Services and Electronic Commerce (LSSI-CE), as well as by Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR) regarding the processing of personal data through cookies.
cookies.s2.title: 2. Types of cookies we use
cookies.s2.intro: On avson.eu we use the following types of cookies:
cookies.s2.type1.text: These are strictly necessary cookies for the functioning of the website. They allow us to remember your privacy preferences (acceptance of the cookie banner) and ensure correct navigation. They do not require consent under art. 22.2 LSSI-CE.
cookies.s2.type2.text: Provided by Google LLC through Google Analytics. They allow us to measure website traffic, user behaviour and content performance. Data is aggregated and anonymised. They require your prior consent.
cookies.s2.type3.text: Google Fonts may set cookies or register font requests. We do not use such cookies for tracking purposes. Fonts are loaded in the context of providing the typographic service.
cookies.s3.title: 3. Cookie table
cookies.s3.intro: The following details the specific cookies used on avson.eu:
cookies.s3.table.header.name: Name
cookies.s3.table.row1.purpose: Stores the preference for acceptance of the cookie banner (localStorage)
cookies.s3.table.row2.purpose: Controls whether the exit-intent modal has already been shown in the current session
cookies.s3.table.row3.purpose: Distinguishes unique users by assigning a randomly generated number as a client identifier
cookies.s3.table.row5.purpose: Distinguishes users. Stores information about how they use the site
cookies.s3.table.row6.purpose: Limits the request rate to the Google Analytics server
cookies.s3.tableNote: (*) Google Analytics cookies are only installed if the user accepts cookies through the consent banner.
cookies.s4.title: 4. How to manage and disable cookies
cookies.s4.intro: You can withdraw your consent or manage cookies at any time through the following means:
cookies.s4.panel.title: Through our cookie panel
cookies.s4.panel.text: Delete the entry cookieAccepted from your localStorage (you can do this from the browser's developer tools, Application tab > Local Storage) for the consent banner to reappear.
cookies.s4.browser1.title: Google Chrome
cookies.s4.browser1.text: Menu (⋮) → Settings → Privacy and security → Cookies and other site data
cookies.s4.browser2.title: Mozilla Firefox
cookies.s4.browser2.text: Menu () → Options → Privacy & Security → Cookies and Site Data
cookies.s4.browser3.title: Apple Safari
cookies.s4.browser3.text: Safari → Preferences → Privacy → Manage Website Data
cookies.s4.browser4.title: Microsoft Edge
cookies.s4.browser4.text: Menu (…) → Settings → Privacy, search and services → Cookies and site permissions
cookies.s4.optout.title: Google Analytics opt-out
cookies.s4.optout.text: You can also install the Google Analytics opt-out browser add-on, which prevents Google Analytics JavaScript from collecting information about your visits to any website.
cookies.s4.warning: Please note that disabling certain cookies may affect the functionality of the website or prevent the proper operation of some sections.
cookies.s5.title: 5. More information
cookies.s5.intro: For any query related to the use of cookies on avson.eu, you can contact us at:
cookies.s5.contact.name: Avson Consultoría Estratégica SLU
cookies.s5.contact.address: Calle Pedro Muñoz Seca 2 (Madrid) · Plaza Francesc Macià 7 (Barcelona)
cookies.s5.more: For more general information about cookies and how they affect you, you can visit the Spanish Data Protection Agency (AEPD) website and the Your Online Choices platform.
cookies.s5.privacidad: Please also see our Privacy Policy for complete information on the processing of personal data.
