consultoria-ens-espana.coverage.label: National coverage
consultoria-ens-espana.coverage.title: Presence nationwide
consultoria-ens-espana.coverage.lead: Avson GRC is not a local consultancy. We are a national team that works with companies from any point in Spain.
consultoria-ens-espana.coverage.msg: Avson GRC is a national consultancy with presence throughout Spain. We work on-site in any city and remotely with the same methodology and the same results.
consultoria-ens-espana.coverage.p1: We have consultants who travel to any Spanish city for meetings, workshops and on-site audits. For implementation and follow-up phases, we use our online collaborative platform, which allows us to manage the project with the same efficiency as if we were in the same office. 70% of our ENS projects are carried out in a mixed modality: key meetings on-site, implementation remotely.
consultoria-ens-espana.coverage.p2: We have certified companies in more than 40 Spanish cities. It doesn't matter if your company is in a provincial capital or a medium-sized city: our team can serve you with the same service standards.
consultoria-ens-espana.diff.label: Differentiators
consultoria-ens-espana.diff.title: Why Avson GRC?
consultoria-ens-espana.diff.lead: We have been certifying companies in ENS for over a decade. We have simplified the process, reduced timelines and guaranteed the result in writing.
consultoria-ens-espana.diff.card1.title: Written success guarantee
consultoria-ens-espana.diff.card1.text: We are the only ENS consultancy in Spain that includes an audit success guarantee in the contract. If you don't pass, we continue working at no additional cost until you obtain the certificate. No small print.
consultoria-ens-espana.diff.card2.title: Proven own methodology
consultoria-ens-espana.diff.card2.text: Our Avson ENS Framework methodology reduces certification timelines by 40% compared to the standard process. We have refined each step with more than 400 completed certifications across all sectors and levels.
consultoria-ens-espana.diff.card3.title: Exclusive GRC specialists
consultoria-ens-espana.diff.card3.text: We are not a law firm or a generalist auditor. Our entire team works exclusively on GRC: ENS, ISO 27001, ISO 22301, DORA and NIS2. That specialisation translates into faster and more secure results for the client.
consultoria-ens-espana.diff.card4.title: Dedicated team for your project
consultoria-ens-espana.diff.card4.text: We assign a principal consultant and a dedicated team from diagnosis to final audit. You won't work with a different team in each phase. Your consultant knows your company from start to finish.
consultoria-ens-espana.levels.label: Certification levels
consultoria-ens-espana.levels.title: ENS Basic vs. Medium vs. High
consultoria-ens-espana.levels.lead: ENS is applied at three security levels. The applicable category depends on the impact an incident would have on the services provided to the public body.
consultoria-ens-espana.levels.r1c2: General ICT providers, SaaS for public administration, consultancies with access to non-critical data
consultoria-ens-espana.levels.r1c3: System integrators, processing platforms, management of sensitive citizen data
consultoria-ens-espana.levels.r1c4: Defence, national security, critical infrastructure, high-impact healthcare systems
consultoria-ens-espana.levels.note: * Indicative costs for medium-sized organisations. The no-obligation quote includes a personalised proposal with no commitment.
consultoria-ens-espana.remote.label: Remote methodology
consultoria-ens-espana.remote.title: How we work remotely — with the same results
consultoria-ens-espana.remote.lead: 70% of our ENS projects are carried out remotely or in a mixed modality, with the same success rates as fully on-site projects. Here's how our process works:
consultoria-ens-espana.remote.step1.title: Diagnosis video call
consultoria-ens-espana.remote.step1.text: We start with a diagnosis session by video call (60-90 min) in which we analyse the scope, applicable ENS category and current security status. We use collaborative tools to complete the initial questionnaire in real time with your team.
consultoria-ens-espana.remote.step2.title: Avson collaborative platform
consultoria-ens-espana.remote.step2.text: The entire project is managed on our online platform: documentation, evidence, action plan, control tracking and direct communication with the consulting team. Your team has 24/7 access to review progress and upload evidence without the need for on-site meetings.
consultoria-ens-espana.remote.step3.title: On-site or documentary audit
consultoria-ens-espana.remote.step3.text: For ENS Basic, the audit can be completely documentary. For ENS Medium and High, we coordinate with the ENAC-accredited certification body the on-site visit (if applicable) or remote audit, which is increasingly accepted by certification bodies.
consultoria-ens-espana.faq.label: FAQ
consultoria-ens-espana.faq.title: Everything you need to know about ENS in Spain
consultoria-ens-espana.faq.q1: What is ENS and who needs it?
consultoria-ens-espana.faq.a1: The National Security Scheme (ENS), regulated by Royal Decree 311/2022, establishes the security requirements that must be met by information systems of public entities and ICT providers that provide services to the public sector. ENS is needed by all companies that bid with the Public Administration in ICT contracts, or that manage data, systems or networks of Spanish public bodies.
consultoria-ens-espana.faq.a2: The usual timeline is 3 to 4 months for most medium-sized organisations, depending on the ENS level (Basic, Medium or High), prior security maturity and size of the organisation. With ENS Express, the timeline can be reduced to 30 days for the Basic level.
consultoria-ens-espana.faq.q2: How long does ENS certification take?
consultoria-ens-espana.faq.q3: How much does ENS consulting cost in Spain?
consultoria-ens-espana.faq.a3: The cost varies according to the ENS level, size of the organisation and starting point. The indicative range goes from €4,000 for ENS Basic in small organisations to €25,000 or more for ENS High in complex organisations. The no-obligation quote includes a personalised economic proposal with no commitment.
consultoria-ens-espana.faq.q4: Do you work throughout Spain or only in Madrid?
consultoria-ens-espana.faq.a4: Avson GRC is a national consultancy with presence throughout Spain. We work on-site in any Spanish city and remotely with the same methodology and the same results. There is no geographical limitation in our service. We have certified clients in more than 40 Spanish cities.
consultoria-ens-espana.faq.q5: What is the difference between ENS Basic, Medium and High?
consultoria-ens-espana.faq.a5: The three ENS levels differ in the potential impact of a security incident on the services provided. The Basic level applies to systems with minor impact; the Medium level to systems with significant impact; and the High level to systems whose interruption or compromise would have serious consequences for the organisation's mission or public safety. The categorisation is determined by the organisation itself with the support of the consultancy.
consultoria-ens-espana.cta.title: Request your no-obligation quote
consultoria-ens-espana.cta.lead: Send us your email and we'll send you a no-obligation quote within 48 hours with the applicable ENS category, certification plan and indicative budget. No commitment.

blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.meta.description: Everything about the ENS certification audit: who can do it, how it unfolds, what auditors check and how not to fail on the first attempt.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.meta.ogTitle: ENS Audit: Step-by-Step Process, Accredited Bodies and How to Prepare | Avson GRC
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.meta.ogDescription: Everything about the ENS certification audit: who can do it, how it unfolds, what auditors check and how not to fail on the first attempt.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.meta.twitterTitle: ENS Audit: Process, Accredited Bodies and Preparation | Avson GRC
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.meta.twitterDescription: Everything about the ENS certification audit: who can do it, phases, what auditors check and how to prepare.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.announce.text: New ENS regulations in force  ·  Request a quote to know which category you need  ·  Request now →
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.hero.label: ENS · Audit · Updated May 2026
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.hero.title: ENS audit: what it is, how it works and how to prepare your company.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.hero.subtitle: The ENS certification audit is the moment of truth. This guide explains exactly what happens in each phase, what auditors look for and how to ensure your company passes on the first attempt.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.hero.author: Laura Sánchez · GRC Consulting Director
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.queEs.title: What is the ENS certification audit
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.queEs.p1: The ENS certification audit is the process by which an independent body accredited by ENAC verifies that an organisation's information systems comply with the requirements of the National Security Scheme established in RD 311/2022. It is the necessary and essential step prior to obtaining the ENS certificate, and its result determines whether the organisation passes or fails the certification process.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.queEs.p2: It is important to distinguish between two types of audit in the context of ENS. The internal audit is carried out by the organisation itself — or a consultancy on its behalf — to assess the state of controls and prepare for certification. It is preparatory and does not produce the certificate. The certification audit is carried out by the ENAC-accredited body, and its positive report enables the issuance of the certificate.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.queEs.p3: The object of the audit is to verify that the organisation has implemented the security measures of Annex II of RD 311/2022 corresponding to the declared category (Basic, Medium or High) and that said measures are effective and sustained in practice, not just on paper. This distinction between "being documented" and "working in practice" is what causes most non-conformities.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.quien.title: Who can perform the ENS audit
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.quien.p1: Only certification bodies accredited by ENAC (National Accreditation Entity) under the specific ENS certification scheme can issue the ENS certificate. ENAC is the national accreditation body in Spain, recognised by Regulation (EC) 765/2008, and accreditation guarantees the technical competence and impartiality of the certification body.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.quien.p2: Among the ENAC-accredited bodies for ENS are some of the leading international certification firms: Bureau Veritas, AENOR, SGS, Applus+ and BSI Group, among others. The CCN publishes an up-to-date list of authorised bodies on its website. The choice of certification body is free, but it is worth considering their experience in the organisation's sector of activity and the available audit timelines.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.quien.p3: An implementation consultancy like Avson GRC cannot issue the ENS certificate because it is not a certification body; its role is to prepare the organisation to pass the audit with the highest probability of success. The relationship between consultancy and certification body is complementary, not substitutive.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.fases.title: Phases of the ENS audit
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.fases.intro: The ENS certification audit is structured into four distinct phases, with different objectives and methodology in each:
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.fases.fase1.title: Phase 1: documentary review
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.fases.fase1.p: Auditors analyse the security management system documentation: Security Policy, risk analysis, Statement of Applicability, Risk Treatment Plan, operating procedures (incident management, backups, access control, patch management, service continuity) and activity records. This phase is usually conducted remotely, before the on-site visit.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.fases.fase2.title: Phase 2: on-site visit (or remote technical audit)
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.fases.fase2.p: Auditors check that documented controls are actually implemented. They interview key personnel (security officer, system administrators, continuity officer), inspect physical facilities (CPD access control, equipment protection, fire and water detection systems), and perform technical checks on systems: security configurations, authentication logs, encryption of data in transit and at rest, vulnerability management and backup restoration tests.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.fases.fase3.title: Phase 3: audit report
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.fases.fase3.p: After the visit, the certification body produces a detailed report with findings: controls that comply, minor non-conformities (deviations that do not affect overall compliance but must be corrected) and major non-conformities (failures that prevent certification until remedied). The organisation has a period — usually 30 to 90 days — to correct non-conformities and submit evidence of remediation.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.fases.fase4.title: Phase 4: certification
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.fases.fase4.p: If the remediation report is satisfactory, the certification body issues the ENS certificate with a validity of 2 years. The certificate is registered in the CCN system and can be publicly verified by public bodies that tender.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.cta1.text: Avson GRC prepares your company for the ENS audit with a 100% success rate. We work with companies nationwide, on-site and remote.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.dimensiones.title: What auditors check in each dimension
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.dimensiones.intro: ENS evaluates systems in five security dimensions, and auditors check specific controls in each of them:
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.dimensiones.list:
• Confidentiality: auditors verify that only authorised persons access information. They check access controls (permission matrices, principle of least privilege), encryption of sensitive data both in transit and at rest, information classification and user management procedures (onboarding, offboarding and permission changes).
  • Integrity: it is verified that information cannot be modified in an unauthorised or inadvertent manner. Auditors check data integrity controls, audit logs (access and modification logs), change management in systems and separation of development and production environments.
  • Availability: the capacity of systems to be operational when needed is verified. Auditors check service continuity plans, frequency and verification of backups, disaster recovery procedures and historical availability metrics.
  • Authenticity: auditors verify that the identity of actors interacting with the system can be confirmed. They check authentication mechanisms (especially multi-factor authentication, mandatory in Medium and High categories), digital certificate management and physical access controls.
  • Traceability: it is verified that all relevant actions on the system are recorded and attributable to a specific user. Auditors check logging systems, log integrity (that they cannot be manipulated), log retention and periodic log review procedures.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.errores.title: The most frequent mistakes that cause non-conformities
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.errores.intro: After supporting more than 90 companies through their ENS certification processes, at Avson GRC we have identified the mistakes that most frequently cause non-conformities in the audit:
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.errores.list:
• Outdated documentation: the Security Policy or risk analysis was prepared more than a year ago and does not reflect significant changes in systems (new servers, cloud migration, new employees). Auditors check the review dates of all documents.
  • Unverified backups: there are documented backup procedures but no actual restoration test has ever been performed. It is one of the most common and serious findings.
  • Poor patch management: systems have known critical unpatched vulnerabilities, or there is no formal security update management process with maximum deadlines.
  • Inadequate access control: users with unnecessary administrator privileges, active ex-employee accounts, lack of periodic permission reviews or weak passwords without a complexity policy.
  • Lack of two-factor authentication: mandatory for privileged access in Medium category and for all remote access. It is a frequent finding in companies with legacy infrastructure.
  • Untested continuity plan: the plan exists on paper but no drill has ever been executed, or the last drill is more than 12 months old.
  • Undocumented security training: staff receive informal training but there is no record of sessions, content or attendees.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.documentacion.title: How to prepare documentation before the audit
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.documentacion.p1: Document preparation is the part of the process over which the organisation has the most control before the audit. A well-prepared and internally consistent set of documents is the best starting point for a successful audit.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.documentacion.p2: The asset inventory must be complete and up to date: hardware, software, data, services and people linked to the system in scope. Each asset must have an assigned owner and a valuation of its criticality in the security dimensions.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.documentacion.p3: The risk analysis must cover relevant threats for the sector and identified assets, with the probability and impact of each threat and the treatment measures applied or planned. The methodology must be recognisable (MAGERIT, ISO 27005 or a documented proprietary methodology) and the results must be updated with respect to the real scope of the system.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.documentacion.p4: The Statement of Applicability must include all controls of Annex II of RD 311/2022 applicable to the category, with their implementation status (implemented, in progress, not applicable with justification) and references to the documentation or evidence demonstrating compliance.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.cta2.text: Have an upcoming ENS audit? We perform a prior simulation to detect any gap before the auditors find it. Available for companies nationwide.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.noSuperas.title: What happens if you don't pass the audit
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.noSuperas.p1: Not passing the audit on the first attempt does not mean definitive failure; it is part of the process for many organisations, especially those certifying for the first time. The remediation procedure is regulated and offers a second chance.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.noSuperas.p2: When the audit detects minor non-conformities — deviations that do not compromise the overall effectiveness of the security system — the organisation can correct them and submit evidence of remediation within the agreed period (usually 30-60 days). If remediation is accepted, the certificate is issued without the need to repeat the full audit.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.noSuperas.p3: When major non-conformities are detected — serious failures that compromise system security — the organisation must remediate them and undergo a new partial or full audit, at the discretion of the certification body. The remediation period is 90 days in most cases.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.noSuperas.p4: If after the remediation period the findings have not been satisfactorily corrected, the certification body closes the file with a negative result and the organisation must begin a new certification process.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.garantia.title: Avson's guarantee: if we don't pass the audit, we repeat at no cost
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.garantia.p1: At Avson GRC we are the only ones in the market to offer a written success guarantee in the certification process. If, following our alignment plan and recommendations, your company does not pass the certification audit, we will prepare you again and repeat the process at no additional cost.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.garantia.p2: This guarantee is not marketing: it is the result of a rigorous work process that includes an exhaustive gap analysis at the start, a detailed and prioritised alignment plan, the preparation of all required documentation, assisted implementation of technical controls, an internal audit modelled on the certification audit, and support during the external audit to answer auditor questions and manage the submission of evidence.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.garantia.p3: We work with companies nationwide, both on-site — travelling to the client's facilities — and fully remotely using collaborative tools. Our experience covers all sectors that work with the Public Administration: technology, healthcare, education, defence, utilities, local government and financial services.

blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.faq.title: Frequently asked questions about the ENS audit
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.faq.q1: How long does the ENS audit last?
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.faq.a1: Duration varies by category and size of the system in scope. For Basic category in an SME, the documentary review and technical visit can be completed in 2-3 working days. For Medium category in a medium-sized company, it can extend to 5-8 working days. The High category typically requires between 2 and 4 weeks of intensive auditing.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.faq.q2: Is the ENS audit done remotely or on-site?
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.faq.a2: Both modalities are valid. The documentary review can always be done remotely. The on-site technical audit can also be done remotely via controlled system access, although for Medium and High categories auditors usually prefer or require an on-site visit to check physical facilities, access control and network segregation.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.faq.q3: What documents must be ready for the ENS audit?
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.faq.a3: Minimum documentation includes: Information Security Policy approved by management, up-to-date risk analysis, Statement of Applicability, Risk Treatment Plan, operational security procedures (incident management, backups, access control, patch management), system audit logs, service continuity plan and staff training records.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.faq.q4: How much does the external ENS audit cost?
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.faq.a4: The cost varies by certification body, category and scope size. As an indicative reference: ENS Basic in an SME, between €3,000 and €6,000; ENS Medium, between €6,000 and €15,000; ENS High, between €15,000 and €40,000 or more. These costs are independent of the implementation consultancy's fees.
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.faq.q5: How often does the ENS audit need to be repeated?
blog/auditoria-ens-proceso.article.faq.a5: The ENS certificate is valid for 2 years, so the renewal audit must be performed before expiry. Additionally, RD 311/2022 requires periodic maintenance audits (at least annually) to verify that controls are maintained at the required level, although these do not replace the biennial certification audit.

blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.meta.description: What is the difference between the ENS Medium and ENS High certificate? Complete comparison of requirements, measures and certification process.

blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.meta.ogDescription: Complete comparison of the three ENS categories: Basic, Medium and High. Requirements, controls and certification process by level.

blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.meta.twitterDescription: Complete comparison of the three ENS categories: Basic, Medium and High. Requirements, controls and certification process by level.
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.announce.text: New ENS regulations in force  ·  Request a quote to know which category you need  ·  Request now →
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.hero.label: ENS · Categories · Updated May 2026
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.hero.title: ENS Medium vs High certificate: differences and how to know which one you need
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.hero.subtitle: The ENS category determines the level of requirement, cost and certification timeline. Choosing the wrong category can invalidate your certificate or make you overpay.
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.tresCategorias.title: The three ENS categories: Basic, Medium and High
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.tresCategorias.p1: The National Security Scheme is not a one-size-fits-all framework. It recognises that not all systems have the same potential impact if compromised and, consequently, establishes three levels of requirement: Basic, Medium and High.
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.tresCategorias.p2: The category of a system is not freely chosen by the company or its consultant: it is determined through a formal asset valuation process according to the methodology set out in Annex I of RD 311/2022. This process analyses the potential impact of a security incident on the five security dimensions of the system: availability, authenticity, integrity, confidentiality and traceability (DAIC-T).
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.tresCategorias.p3: If the impact in any of those dimensions is assessed as low, the system is Basic. If any dimension reaches medium impact, the system is Medium category. If high or very high impact is reached, the category is High.
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.tresCategorias.p4: The category of the system determines which measures from the catalogue of Annex II of RD 311/2022 must be applied and with what level of intensity (low, medium or high). The higher the category, the greater the number of measures and the higher the required level of implementation.
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.basica.title: ENS Basic certificate: when it applies and what measures it requires
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.basica.p1: The ENS Basic category applies when the potential impact of a security incident on the system is limited. In practice, this corresponds to systems handling not particularly sensitive information, not supporting essential services and whose interruption would not cause serious harm to citizens or the Administration.
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.basica.p2: Typical examples of systems in the Basic category: document management applications with non-sensitive administrative data, internal information systems supporting administrative activity, informational web portals, registry management systems without sensitive personal data.
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.basica.p3: For ENS Basic, the catalogue of measures requires the implementation of controls at their low level (L). The most relevant measures include:
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.basica.list:
• Information Security Policy approved by management
  • Documented basic risk analysis
  • Access control with strong username and password authentication
  • Access privilege management (principle of least privilege)
  • Protection against malicious code (antivirus, EDR)
  • Backups and verified restoration procedure
  • Basic incident management with logging
  • Basic information security training for personnel
  • Documented asset inventory
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.basica.p4: An important advantage of the Basic category is that it allows the Declaration of Conformity as an accreditation mechanism: the company can self-declare its conformity without the need for an external audit. However, as explained, more and more public tenders require the full certificate issued by an ENAC-accredited body even for the Basic category.
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.cta1.text: Don't know which category you need? Avson's no-obligation quote determines it precisely within 5 working days.
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.media.title: ENS Medium certificate: who needs it and which controls it includes
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.media.p1: The ENS Medium category is the most common among medium and large Spanish public sector ICT provider companies. It applies when the potential impact of a security incident in any dimension of the system is serious.
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.media.p2: Examples of systems in the Medium category: electronic processing platforms with citizens' personal data, administrative file management systems, public procurement platforms, systems supporting public health or education services, cloud infrastructure hosting personal data subject to the GDPR.
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.media.p3: For ENS Medium, the catalogue measures must be applied at their medium level (M), which implies significantly more demanding controls than the low level:
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.media.list:
• Mandatory two-factor authentication (MFA) for access to systems with sensitive data and remote access
  • Formal risk analysis with a recognised methodology (compatible with PILAR, MAGERIT or similar)
  • Up-to-date and approved Risk Treatment Plan (PTR)
  • Vulnerability management with periodic analysis (at least quarterly) and patching plan
  • Continuous security monitoring with SIEM or equivalent system
  • Privileged access management (PAM) for system administrators
  • Configuration management with documented security baselines
  • Formalised and tested Service Continuity Plan
  • Incident management with response team and documented procedures
  • Access auditing for critical systems with log retention
  • Encryption of data in transit and at rest for sensitive information
  • Documented physical facility access control
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.media.p4: The ENS Medium certification process mandatorily requires an audit by an ENAC-accredited body. It is not possible to opt for a Declaration of Conformity. The audit includes exhaustive documentary review and technical checks of implemented controls.
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.alta.title: ENS High certificate: for critical infrastructure
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.alta.p1: The ENS High category is the most demanding of the scheme and applies when the potential impact of a security incident would be very serious or catastrophic. It is typical of critical infrastructure designated by the national operator, defence and security systems, essential state services (energy, water, transport), information systems handling especially sensitive data such as classified information.
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.alta.p2: For ENS High, all measures in the catalogue are applied at their high level (H), the most demanding:
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.alta.list:
• Multi-factor authentication with digital certificates or hardware tokens (not just TOTP software)
  • Physical network segregation for high-criticality systems
  • High-strength cryptography with algorithms approved by the CCN
  • Regular penetration testing (pentesting) with findings correction
  • Incident management with advanced response capabilities (internal or contracted CSIRT)
  • Forensic analysis of security incidents
  • Removable device and storage media control with mandatory encryption
  • Security code review for critical applications
  • Continuity Plan with documented and regularly tested RTO and RPO
  • Security assessment of supply chain providers
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.determinar.title: How to determine your company's ENS category
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.determinar.p1: The formal category determination process follows these steps:
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.determinar.list:
• Scope identification: determine which information systems fall within the certification scope. The scope must be clearly defined and justified.
  • Asset inventory: identify all assets that are part of the system in scope: information, services, software, hardware, networks, facilities and personnel.
  • Asset valuation: assign to each asset a value in the five security dimensions (DAIC-T) using the scale of Annex I of RD 311/2022. The PILAR tool from the CCN greatly facilitates this process.
  • Category determination: the system category is the highest of the individual categories of its component assets. If the most critical asset is Medium, the system is Medium.
  • Documentation: the entire process must be documented in the Applicability Document and the Declaration of Applicability of ENS measures.
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.determinar.p2: A common mistake is trying to manipulate the valuation to obtain a lower category and reduce implementation effort. Audit bodies are experts at detecting inconsistent valuations, and a certificate issued for an incorrect category can be challenged.
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.diferencias.title: Differences in the certification process by category
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.diferencias.table.riesgos.label: Depth of risk analysis
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.diferencias.table.mfa.label: MFA mandatory
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.diferencias.table.siem.label: SIEM monitoring
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.diferencias.table.vigencia.label: Certificate validity
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.costeTiempo.title: Cost and time by category
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.costeTiempo.p1: The cost and timeline of certification vary significantly by category and organisation size. As a general reference:
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.costeTiempo.p2: ENS Basic: the most accessible certification. For an SME of 10-30 employees with a limited scope to a specific system, the process can be completed in 2-3 months. The number of measures and depth required are lower, reducing both implementation effort and audit cost.
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.costeTiempo.p3: ENS Medium: the most common and balanced process. For a company of 20-100 employees, the typical process lasts between 3 and 4 months. The greater complexity compared to Basic comes mainly from technical controls (MFA, SIEM, vulnerability management) and the depth of the audit. The cost is moderate for the value it provides in terms of access to public contracts.
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.costeTiempo.p4: ENS High: the most demanding and expensive certification. It is usually required in organisations with complex infrastructure and high-criticality systems. The typical timeline is 4-6 months and the cost is significantly higher due to the depth of technical controls and audit complexity.
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.cta2.text: Cascade certifications: ENS Medium + ISO 27001 at 65% of the cost thanks to overlapping requirements.

blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.faq.title: FAQ
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.faq.q1: How do I know if I need ENS Medium or High?
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.faq.a1: The category is determined by assessing the potential impact of a security incident on the systems in scope using the scale of Annex I of RD 311/2022. If the impact in any dimension would be serious, Medium applies; if very serious or catastrophic, High applies. The PILAR tool from the CCN facilitates this assessment. If in doubt, Avson's no-obligation quote determines the correct category.
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.faq.q2: Can I get certified in Medium if the tender only requires Basic?
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.faq.a2: Yes, and it can be advantageous. A higher category certification covers the lower category requirements. Additionally, some tenders positively score higher category certifications, which can give you a competitive advantage in the technical evaluation of the bid.
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.faq.q3: How much longer does ENS High certification take compared to Medium?
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.faq.a3: An ENS High project may require 1 to 2 additional months compared to Medium, mainly due to the greater technical complexity of some controls (advanced cryptography, network segregation, penetration testing) and the greater depth of the certification audit.
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.faq.q4: Is it possible to upgrade the category after certification?
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.faq.a4: Yes. If the company wins contracts requiring a higher category, it can expand the scope or category of its certification. This requires a new audit, but the previous work is largely reusable, reducing the additional effort.
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.faq.q5: Is ENS Medium compatible with ISO 27001?
blog/certificado-ens-media-alta.article.faq.a5: Yes, and they are highly synergistic. ENS Medium and ISO 27001 share approximately 65% of their requirements. A company certified in ENS Medium already has most of the controls required by ISO 27001 implemented, allowing it to obtain this second certification at a significantly lower marginal cost.

blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.hero.label: ENS · Mandatory nature · Updated May 2026
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.hero.title: ENS is mandatory: does your company need to get certified?
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.hero.subtitle: Many companies discover they need ENS only after they have lost a tender. This guide explains precisely who it affects, which contracts require it and what happens if you don't have it.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.hero.author: By the Avson GRC team
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.queCambio.title: ENS has been mandatory since 2022: what changed?
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.queCambio.p1: Royal Decree 311/2022, of 3 May, approving the National Security Scheme, marked a turning point from the previous regulations. If RD 3/2010 already established the mandatory nature of ENS for Public Administrations, RD 311/2022 did two key things that directly affect private companies.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.queCambio.p2: First, it explicitly extended the scope to private suppliers of ICT systems and services. Before, the obligation fell mainly on the administrations themselves; now, any private company providing technology services to the public sector is equally obliged to comply with ENS for the systems affected by that service provision.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.queCambio.p3: Second, it established a 24-month transitional period for organisations to adapt to the new requirements. That period ended in May 2024. From that moment on, there is no margin for tolerance: companies without an ENS certificate that provide ICT services to the Administration are in a situation of legal non-compliance.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.queCambio.callout: Key fact: the transitional period of RD 311/2022 ended in May 2024. There is no additional extension mechanism. Companies without ENS operating with the Public Administration are in non-compliance today.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.obligadas.title: Which companies are obliged to get ENS certified
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.obligadas.p1: Article 2 of RD 311/2022 delimits the scope. Beyond the Public Administrations themselves, the affected private companies are those that:
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.obligadas.list:
• Provide information and communication technology services to Spanish public sector bodies. This includes software development, application maintenance, cloud services (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS), infrastructure management, cybersecurity services, telecommunications and any other ICT service.
  • Supply technology solutions for information systems of Public Administrations, even if they do not directly manage those systems.
  • Provide communications systems used by public sector bodies.
  • Handle, store or transmit data owned or under the responsibility of a Public Administration, even partially or temporarily.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.obligadas.p2: In practice, this affects a very wide range of companies: technology consultancies, system integrators, ISVs (independent software vendors), managed service providers (MSPs), hosting and cloud companies, telecommunications operators with public contracts and technology training companies for public employees, among others.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.obligadas.p3: The question every company should ask is: Is any of our clients a public sector body and does the service we provide involve access to, processing or management of their information systems or data? If the answer is yes, ENS applies to you.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.cta1.text: Unsure if ENS applies to you? Within 5 working days we'll give you a clear and free answer.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.contratos.title: What type of public contracts require the ENS certificate
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.contratos.p1: ENS appears as a requirement in Public Administration procurement specifications in various forms. The most common is as:
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.contratos.p2: Eligibility or technical solvency requirement: the administrative clauses specifications require the bidder to demonstrate compliance with ENS in the category applicable to the contract object. Without this accreditation, the bid is excluded during the prior requirement verification phase, even before the price or technical proposal is evaluated.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.contratos.p3: Contract execution condition: in some cases, ENS is not required at the bidding stage but as a condition to start or maintain service provision. The contractor must obtain the certification within a deadline set in the specification or contract itself.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.contratos.p4: Evaluation criterion: less frequent but possible, ENS (or its category) can be used as an evaluation criterion that awards additional points to bidders certified in a higher category than the minimum required.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.contratos.p5: Contracts that most frequently include ENS requirements are ICT services in general (development, maintenance, support), cloud services contracts for the Administration, telecommunications contracts, cybersecurity and monitoring services, and technology consulting and digital transformation contracts for the public sector.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.contratos.p6: The Public Sector Procurement Platform (PLACE) publishes all specifications. An analysis of contracts awarded in the last two years in ICT categories shows that more than 78% of contracts above €50,000 include some ENS requirement.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.consecuencias.title: Consequences of not having ENS: lost contracts and penalties
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.consecuencias.p1: The consequences of ENS non-compliance are multiple and have different degrees of severity:
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.consecuencias.p2: Exclusion from tenders: it is the most immediate and frequent consequence. If the specification requires the ENS certificate and you don't provide it, your bid is automatically excluded. There is no possibility to rectify this requirement once the submission deadline has closed.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.consecuencias.p3: Termination of existing contracts: if you have active contracts with Public Administrations and you do not comply with ENS, the contracting body can initiate a non-compliance procedure that may result in contract termination, with the corresponding economic penalties.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.consecuencias.p4: Impossibility of renewing contracts: public contracts are renewed periodically. In the renewal or extension phase, more and more Administrations verify the contractor's ENS compliance. Without the certificate, renewal can be denied.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.consecuencias.p5: Liability in the event of security incidents: if a security incident affects Administration data or systems managed by a supplier without ENS, the supplier's liability is significantly aggravated. Lack of certification can be considered negligence in fulfilling contractual and legal obligations.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.consecuencias.p6: Reputational impact: in a market where more and more companies are certified, not having ENS generates distrust. Procurement departments of Public Administrations — and of large private companies that require ENS from their suppliers — perceive it as a sign of insufficient security maturity.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.cta2.text: Every month without ENS is a potential contract lost. The process with Avson takes 3-4 months.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.subcontratista.title: What if I am a subcontractor to a certified company?
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.subcontratista.p1: This is a very common doubt among companies that participate in public projects as first or second level subcontractors. The answer depends on several factors:
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.subcontratista.p2: If as a subcontractor you access, handle, store or process data or systems of the Public Administration — even indirectly or partially — ENS also applies to you. The security custody chain extends along the entire supply chain.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.subcontratista.p3: In practice, the main contractor (the ENS-certified company) is responsible for ensuring that its subcontractors also comply with applicable security requirements. More and more public contracts include clauses obliging the main contractor to require ENS from its critical subcontractors.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.subcontratista.p4: The best way to protect your position as a subcontractor — and to win new opportunities as one — is to obtain your own ENS certificate. This makes you more attractive to certified companies that need to subcontract, because it simplifies the chain of security responsibilities.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.plazos.title: Deadlines: when do you need to have the certificate?
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.plazos.p1: The short answer is: right now. The transitional period of RD 311/2022 ended in May 2024. Since that date, there is no additional adaptation period.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.plazos.p2: However, the practical reality is somewhat more nuanced. There are three different situations:
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.plazos.p3: Companies with active contracts without ENS: technically they are in non-compliance. The speed with which the contracting body acts varies, but the risk of a requirement or contract termination exists and is growing. The solution is to start the certification process immediately.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.plazos.p4: Companies that will bid shortly: if the specification requires ENS, you need the certificate before the bid submission date. Bidding processes are usually announced with 15-30 days' notice. With 3-4 months of certification process, you need to start long before the tender you are interested in is published.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.plazos.p5: Companies that don't yet have public contracts but want them: the optimal time to get certified is before the opportunity arises, not after. The opportunity cost of losing a tender because you don't have ENS is almost always higher than the cost of certification.

blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.faq.title: FAQ
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.faq.a1: ENS has been mandatory since the approval of RD 3/2010, but it was RD 311/2022 that extended the obligation to private providers and established a 24-month transitional period that ended in May 2024. Since that date, there is no additional margin.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.faq.q2: What happens if I don't have ENS and I submit to a tender?
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.faq.a2: Your bid will be automatically excluded during the minimum requirement verification phase. ENS appears as a knockout requirement in the technical specifications of most ICT contracts with the Spanish Public Administration. There is no possibility of subsequent rectification.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.faq.q3: Do I have to get certified if I am only a subcontractor?
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.faq.a3: It depends on whether you access Administration data or systems. If you do, ENS applies to you. Additionally, main contractors increasingly require ENS from their subcontractors to comply with their own contractual obligations. Having ENS is an important competitive advantage in the public subcontracting chain.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.faq.q4: Which ENS category do I need?
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.faq.a4: The category (Basic, Medium or High) depends on the assessment of the potential impact of a security incident on the systems in scope. Most ICT SMEs providing services to the public sector need ENS Basic or Medium. Avson's no-obligation quote determines exactly which category applies to you.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.faq.q5: Can I use a Declaration of Conformity instead of the certificate?
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.faq.a5: Only if your systems are Basic category and the specific tender accepts it. More and more Administrations require the full certificate issued by an ENAC-accredited body, even for Basic category. If in doubt, the safest option is to obtain the full certificate.
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.faq.q6: How long does it take to obtain ENS if I start now?
blog/certificado-ens-obligatorio.article.faq.a6: With Avson GRC, the full process from initial diagnosis to certificate issuance takes between 3 and 4 months. The timeline depends mainly on system category, number of systems in scope and the availability of the internal team to implement controls and generate evidence.

blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.porQueNadie.title: Why does almost no one publish ENS prices?
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.porQueNadie.p1: If you've been looking for information on the cost of ENS certification, you will have noticed one thing: almost no one publishes prices. Websites of consultancies with entire pages dedicated to ENS that always end with "contact us for a personalised quote". This article is going to be different.
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.porQueNadie.p2: The opacity of prices in the ENS consulting market has several causes. The first is legitimate: the cost genuinely varies depending on factors such as the ENS category, the size of the organisation, the number of systems in scope and the level of starting security maturity. The second cause is less edifying: lack of transparency allows adjusting the price to the client's apparent budget, not the real value of the service.
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.porQueNadie.p3: At Avson we bet on transparency. The ranges we publish here are the real ranges we work with in 2025 and 2026. They may vary in each case, but they are an honest reference that will allow you to budget and compare.
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.factores.title: Factors that determine the price of the ENS certificate
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.factores.intro: Before the numbers, it is important to understand which factors have the greatest impact on the cost of an ENS project:
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.factores.list:
• ENS category (Basic, Medium or High): The number of mandatory controls grows significantly with category. ENS Basic has the lightest requirements; ENS High requires the highest level of technical and documentary implementation.
  • Size of the organisation: The number of employees, locations and systems in scope determines the volume of implementation and documentation work.
  • Starting security maturity: A company with no previous security policy requires much more work than one that already has ISO 27001 or has previously worked on security.
  • Number of systems in scope: ENS applies to the systems that process or support services for the Administration. The more systems, the larger the scope and, therefore, the work.
  • Technical complexity: Complex cloud environments, distributed architectures or legacy systems increase the analysis work and implementation of technical controls.
  • Urgency: Urgent projects (need for certificate in less than 2 months) may require intensive dedication with a surcharge.

blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.rangos.title: Real price ranges for ENS consulting
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.rangos.intro: The following ranges correspond to the cost of preparation consulting (gap analysis, control implementation, documentation, training and audit support). They do not include the cost of the external audit, which is detailed in the next section.
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.rangos.p1: These ranges assume an organisation with no prior security certifications. If the company already has ISO 27001, costs are reduced by between 40% and 60% depending on the category and the verified overlap in the initial gap analysis.
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.rangos.p2: The wider ranges (especially for large companies and ENS High) reflect the great variability in technical complexity, number of systems and starting state that exists between organisations of similar size. The initial diagnosis is the only reliable method to budget accurately.
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.incluye.title: What the ENS consulting price includes
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.incluye.intro: A well-executed ENS project includes the following phases and deliverables:
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.incluye.list:
• Gap analysis: Assessment of the current security status against the ENS requirements for the target category. Deliverable: prioritised gap report.
  • Scope definition and security policy: Determination of systems in scope and preparation of the Security Policy approved by management.
  • Risk analysis: Identification of assets, threats and vulnerabilities according to a methodology compatible with ENS (MAGERIT or equivalent). Deliverable: risk analysis report and treatment plan.
  • Normative documentation: Preparation of all policies, procedures and security standards required by ENS: access control policy, incident management, continuity, system acquisition, cryptography, etc.
  • Technical control implementation: Support in the implementation of technical controls (system configuration, hardening, network segmentation, identity management, etc.) or verification of their correct implementation if performed by the internal team.
  • Continuity and recovery plan: For ENS Medium and High, preparation or review of the service continuity plan and disaster recovery plan.
  • Staff training: Security awareness session for personnel in scope and specific technical training for the IT team.
  • Internal audit and evidence preparation: Internal review prior to the certification audit and preparation of the evidence dossier for the external auditor.
  • Audit support: Presence during the certification audit to facilitate client responses and resolve auditor queries.
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.costesAdicionales.title: What additional costs are there
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.costesAdicionales.intro: The total budget for ENS certification has three different components that it is important to know before making decisions:
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.costesAdicionales.p1: 1. Cost of the external audit (certification body): The ENS certification audit must be carried out by a certification body accredited by ENAC under the specific ENS scheme. The cost varies depending on the body and the size of the organisation. Approximate reference for SMEs: between €2,500 and €8,000 for ENS Basic/Medium; up to €15,000-€20,000 for ENS High in medium organisations. The main bodies operating in Spain include AENOR, Bureau Veritas, TÜV Rheinland, SGS and BDO.
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.costesAdicionales.p2: 2. Tools and software licences: Depending on the controls to be implemented and the company's starting technological state, it may be necessary to purchase or expand software licences: identity management solutions (IdM/IAM), antivirus/EDR, backup tools, SIEM solutions for ENS High, etc. This cost is highly variable: it can be zero if the company already has the necessary technology stack, or several thousand euros if significant technology investments are required.
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.costesAdicionales.p3: 3. Internal time of the client's team: Often the most underestimated cost. The certification process requires dedication of the client's technical and management staff: analysis meetings, control implementation, documentation approval, training. We estimate between 40 and 120 hours of internal dedication for an SME, depending on the category and the level of delegation to the consultancy.

blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.costeNo.title: The cost of NOT having ENS: lost contracts
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.costeNo.p1: Calculating the cost of ENS consulting in the abstract is an incomplete exercise. The right question is not "how much does ENS cost?" but "how much does not having ENS cost me?"
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.costeNo.p2: The impact of not having an ENS certificate when needed is twofold and measurable:
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.costeNo.p3: Contracts lost due to direct exclusion: Any public procurement specification that requires ENS as a knockout requirement means that the company is automatically excluded without it. In the ICT market with the Spanish Public Administration, where contracts can range from €50,000 to several million, a single exclusion can represent an opportunity cost far higher than the consulting cost.
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.costeNo.p4: Loss of advantage in evaluation criteria: In tenders where ENS is a scoring criterion (not knockout), not having it means a penalty in the technical evaluation that can make the difference between winning and losing a contract against a certified competitor.
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.costeNo.p5: A useful exercise: if your company currently invoices €500,000 annually to the public sector or aspires to do so, and estimates that ENS would allow it to access additional opportunities equivalent to 20% of that volume, the annual value of the certificate is €100,000 in potential new revenue. With that data, the cost of consulting is amortised in a matter of weeks.
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.roi.title: ROI of the ENS certificate: how to calculate it
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.roi.intro: The calculation of the return on investment of ENS is simpler than it seems. A basic formula:
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.roi.list:
• Step 1: Estimate the volume of public ICT contracts you aspire to in the next 24 months for which ENS is a requirement or relevant evaluation criterion.
  • Step 2: Calculate the gross margin of those contracts (revenue × estimated margin).
  • Step 3: Estimate the incremental probability of winning those contracts if you have ENS vs. if you don't. A conservative estimate: from 0% to 30-40% probability in tenders where it is a knockout requirement, or from +10 to +20 points in tenders where it is an evaluation criterion.
  • Step 4: Additional expected value = Volume × Margin × Incremental probability.
  • Step 5: Compare that expected value with the total cost of certification (consulting + audit + renewal). If the ratio is greater than 3:1 in the first two years, ENS is a profitable investment with high probability.
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.roi.p1: In practice, most of our clients recover the investment in the first public contract they win thanks to the ENS certificate. Multi-year maintenance contracts and framework agreements mean that the value generated by the certificate multiplies year after year during its validity.
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.garantia.title: Why Avson's success guarantee changes the calculation
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.garantia.p1: A part of the ENS consulting market operates without guarantees: they present a proposal, collect the project fee, and if the audit fails, the client has to pay again for a second attempt. This turns the cost of consulting into a variable figure with upward risk.
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.garantia.p2: At Avson, we offer something that practically no other consultancy on the market offers: a written success guarantee. If the project does not end in certification, we refund our fees. This guarantee radically changes the risk calculation for the client:
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.garantia.list:
• The maximum project cost is known from the start: no surprises or additional costs for retries.
  • The guarantee is an indicator of confidence in our methodology: it can only be offered if the team has the knowledge and experience to guarantee the result.
  • It eliminates timeline risk: without a guarantee, a project that fails the audit can be delayed an additional 6-12 months, with the opportunity cost of contracts lost during that time.
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.garantia.p3: Combined with our 3 to 4 month timeline (compared to the market's 6-12 months), the guarantee turns ENS certification into an investment decision with controlled risk and predictable return.

blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.faq.title: Frequently asked questions about the price of ENS
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.faq.q1: Is the external audit included in the price?
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.faq.a1: It depends on the consultancy. At Avson, the preparation consulting and the external audit are separate contracts, allowing the client to choose their preferred certification body. The external audit with an ENAC-accredited body has an additional cost that typically ranges between €2,500 and €8,000 for SMEs, depending on the ENS category and chosen certification body. We always specify this clearly before signing the contract.
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.faq.q2: Are there grants or subsidies for ENS?
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.faq.a2: There is currently no specific state grant line for ENS certification in private companies. However, some autonomous communities (especially Madrid, Catalonia and the Basque Country) have digitalisation and cybersecurity programmes that may partially cover security consulting costs. The Kit Digital (ACELERA PYME programme) can finance cybersecurity services that are part of the ENS process in some cases. We advise on these options in each project.
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.faq.q3: Does the price change if I already have ISO 27001?
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.faq.a3: Yes, significantly. A company with ISO 27001 already has approximately 65% of ENS controls implemented. This reduces consulting work by a corresponding proportion. Depending on the target ENS category and the level of ISO 27001 implementation, the cost of ENS consulting can be reduced by 40% to 60% compared to starting from scratch. This is one of the most powerful arguments for doing ENS + ISO 27001 jointly or sequentially.
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.faq.q4: Can I finance the ENS consulting?
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.faq.a4: Avson offers the possibility to split payment into milestones tied to project progress. This avoids a large upfront payment and aligns payments with value generation. Contact us to learn about payment options tailored to your size and situation.
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.faq.q5: Why is there such a difference in prices between consultancies?
blog/cuanto-cuesta-certificado-ens.article.faq.a5: The price difference between ENS consultancies reflects real differences in the value proposition: some offer generic documentation and little real technical implementation work; others, like Avson, offer full implementation with a success guarantee. The experience of the consulting team, the number of hours dedicated, the inclusion or not of tools, and the historical success rate also influence. The lowest price is rarely the lowest total cost once audit retries and lost time are taken into account.

blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.hero.label: ENS · Certification · Updated May 2026
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.hero.title: ENS Declaration of Conformity vs ENS certificate: they are not the same thing.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.hero.subtitle: Many companies confuse these two documents and submit the declaration when the tender requires the certificate. The result is exclusion from the tender. This guide explains the differences precisely.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.hero.author: Laura Sánchez · GRC Consulting Director
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.queEsDeclaracion.title: What is the ENS Declaration of Conformity
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.queEsDeclaracion.p1: The ENS Declaration of Conformity is a document prepared and signed by the organisation itself stating that its information systems comply with the requirements of the National Security Scheme. It is, in essence, a self-declaration: it does not require any independent third party to verify that controls are actually implemented.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.queEsDeclaracion.p2: RD 311/2022 contemplates this mechanism exclusively for Basic category systems. To validly issue it, the organisation must have previously carried out a risk analysis, implemented the security measures of Annex II of RD 311/2022 applicable to the Basic category, and documented all of this in a coherent and verifiable manner.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.queEsDeclaracion.p3: The preparation process includes: identifying the systems in scope, valuing the assets and determining the category (which must result as Basic), implementing the required controls, documenting the status of each control and signing the declaration before the organisation's security officer. The declaration must include an explicit reference to RD 311/2022, a description of the system in scope and the date of preparation.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.queEsDeclaracion.p4: From a legal point of view, the Declaration of Conformity has the same value as any other responsible declaration in the Spanish administrative system: the signatory assumes responsibility for its veracity and may incur administrative or criminal liability if it is false. It is not a minor document or an empty formality.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.queEsCertificado.title: What is the ENS certificate
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.queEsCertificado.p1: The ENS certificate is the document issued by a certification body accredited by ENAC (National Accreditation Entity) that certifies that the audited information system complies with the requirements of ENS in the declared category and dimensions. Unlike the Declaration of Conformity, the certificate requires an independent audit carried out by external professionals who objectively and impartially verify the real state of the controls.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.queEsCertificado.p2: The certification process includes two audit phases: the documentary review (in which auditors analyse the security policy, risk analysis, procedures and control records) and the on-site or remote technical audit (in which implemented technical controls are checked: configurations, access, logs, encryption, continuity). Once the audit is completed with no open non-conformities, the body issues the certificate.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.queEsCertificado.p3: The ENS certificate has a validity of 2 years from the date of issue. After that period, it must be renewed through a new audit. Among the ENAC-accredited bodies for issuing ENS certificates are Bureau Veritas, AENOR, SGS, Applus+ and BSI, among others. The certificate explicitly identifies the certified organisation, systems in scope, category, security dimensions evaluated, date of issue and expiry date, and can be verified in the CCN public register.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.tabla.title: Key differences: comparison table
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.cta1.text: Does your tender require a certificate or declaration? Avson GRC analyses your case in 24 hours. We work with companies nationwide, on-site and remote.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.quePiden.title: What do tenders actually require?
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.quePiden.p1: The actual practice of public procurement in Spain shows an unambiguous trend towards the certificate issued by an accredited body, even for Basic category systems. This trend has accelerated since the entry into force of RD 311/2022 and the transitional periods that have already ended.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.quePiden.p2: Ministries and bodies of the General State Administration, large public hospitals, public universities and the most digitally mature autonomous communities systematically require the accredited body certificate in their specifications. Local administration is more heterogeneous, but large municipalities also tend towards the certificate.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.quePiden.p3: Tenders usually refer to the ENS certificate using expressions such as: "ENS certificate issued by a certification body accredited by ENAC", "ENS conformity certification issued by an independent third party" or "ENS security certificate". When the expression "Declaration of Conformity" appears explicitly, that mechanism is usually accepted for the Basic category, but it is increasingly less frequent.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.quePiden.p4: A very common mistake is to read the specification quickly and assume that any "ENS document" is sufficient. The most modern specifications are explicit: they require the certificate and specify that the issuing body must be accredited by ENAC. Submitting only the Declaration of Conformity in those cases is grounds for automatic exclusion.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.cuandoSentido.title: When does the Declaration of Conformity make sense?
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.cuandoSentido.p1: The Declaration of Conformity makes sense in a limited number of situations. The first scenario is the company that wants to establish an internal compliance base before starting the certification process: the declaration acts as an intermediate objective that forces the organisation to implement controls and document them, facilitating the transition to the certificate.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.cuandoSentido.p2: The second scenario is contracts with small public bodies — some rural municipalities, smaller local entities — that still do not require the formal certificate and accept the declaration. It is important to check case by case, because the trend is towards the certificate.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.cuandoSentido.p3: The third scenario is extreme urgency: a company that has a contract in process with a very tight deadline and whose controls are implemented but has not yet undergone the external audit. In that case, the declaration can be a temporary solution, provided the specification explicitly accepts it.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.cuandoSentido.p4: What never makes sense is to use the Declaration of Conformity as a permanent substitute for the certificate when the company intends to be a regular bidder. The cost of losing a single important tender far exceeds the cost of obtaining the certificate.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.riesgos.title: Risks of submitting the declaration when the tender requires the certificate
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.riesgos.p1: Submitting a Declaration of Conformity in a tender process that requires the certificate has concrete consequences. The first risk is direct exclusion: the procurement board will disqualify the bid for not meeting the minimum technical requirements of the specification. There is no appeal if the specification was clear.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.riesgos.p2: The second risk is loss of renewal contracts: if the company already has an active contract and the body updates its security requirements requiring the certificate at renewal, the company can lose that renewal. This is more common than it seems in long-term IT maintenance and support contracts.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.riesgos.p3: The third risk affects reputation: in a relatively small market of ICT providers to the Administration, an exclusion due to non-compliance with regulatory requirements generates a negative perception that can affect future commercial relationships.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.riesgos.p4: Finally, there is a legal risk: if the company submits a Declaration of Conformity that does not faithfully reflect the state of its controls, it incurs a false declaration with potential administrative and, in extreme cases, criminal consequences.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.cta2.text: More than 90 companies already have their ENS certificate with Avson. Average time: 3.5 months. Written success guarantee. Nationwide.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.comoObtener.title: How to obtain the ENS certificate with a guarantee
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.comoObtener.p1: The process to go from zero — or from an existing Declaration of Conformity — to the ENS certificate has well-defined phases. The starting point is the GAP analysis, which assesses the current state of controls against the ENS requirements for the applicable category and quantifies the remaining work.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.comoObtener.p2: With the results of the gap analysis, a detailed alignment plan is prepared covering the necessary documentation — security policy, risk analysis, operating procedures, continuity plan, incident management — and the implementation of technical controls: two-factor authentication, encryption, monitoring, patch management and verified backups.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.comoObtener.p3: Before the certification audit, an internal pre-audit is carried out to identify and correct any deviations. This is the dress rehearsal that ensures the organisation arrives at the external audit without surprises.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.comoObtener.p4: At Avson GRC we accompany the entire process from the initial diagnosis to the receipt of the certificate, working with companies nationwide both on-site and remotely. Our guarantee is simple: if following our alignment plan you do not pass the audit, we repeat it at no additional cost. It is a guarantee we have given in more than 90 projects and which, to date, we have not had to execute.

blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.faq.title: Frequently asked questions about the ENS Declaration of Conformity
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.faq.q1: Can a company of any size make the Declaration of Conformity?
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.faq.a1: Yes. RD 311/2022 does not establish any size requirement to issue the Declaration of Conformity. Any company that has implemented the ENS controls for the Basic category can prepare it. However, size does not exempt from the responsibility that the controls are actually implemented: the declaration is a legal assertion that can be verified in a later audit.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.faq.q2: Does the Declaration of Conformity have an expiry date?
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.faq.a2: RD 311/2022 does not set an explicit maximum validity for the Declaration of Conformity, but requires it to be kept up to date and reflect the real state of the system. Any significant change in the system forces a review and update of the declaration. Many organisations review it at least annually.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.faq.q3: Do specifications always state whether they want a certificate or a declaration?
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.faq.a3: Not always with the desired clarity. Some specifications use ambiguous expressions such as 'ENS accreditation' or 'ENS compliance' without specifying. In those cases, the most prudent thing is to ask the contracting body to clarify whether it accepts the Declaration of Conformity or requires the accredited body certificate. Submitting the declaration when the certificate is required is grounds for exclusion.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.faq.q4: If I have the declaration, can I easily convert it into a certificate?
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.faq.a4: If the declaration was prepared correctly and the controls are actually implemented, the path to the certificate is much shorter than starting from scratch. What the certification process adds is the audit by an ENAC-accredited body. With Avson GRC, companies starting from a solid declaration typically take between 6 and 8 weeks to obtain the certificate.
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.faq.q5: Is the Declaration of Conformity valid for ENS Medium?
blog/declaracion-conformidad-ens.article.faq.a5: No. The Declaration of Conformity is only valid for Basic category systems. For Medium and High categories, RD 311/2022 mandatorily requires an audit by an ENAC-accredited body and the formal certificate. There is no self-declaration option for these categories.

blog/dora-entidades-financieras.hero.subtitle: Banks, insurers, fintech and asset managers have specific obligations under DORA from January 2025. This guide explains what the regulation requires of each type of entity, what TLPT tests are and how to structure compliance.
blog/dora-entidades-financieras.hero.author: By the Avson GRC team
