blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.meta.title: Renewal of the ENS Certificate: Deadlines and Process | Avson
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.meta.description: The ENS certificate expires every 2 years. Know the renewal deadlines, the audit process and how to keep your certification valid without surprises.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.meta.ogTitle: Renewal of the ENS Certificate: everything you need to know | Avson GRC
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.meta.ogDescription: The ENS certificate expires every 2 years. Deadlines, audit process and ongoing maintenance explained.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.announce.text: Is your ENS renewal approaching?  ·  Plan 3-4 months in advance · No surprises  ·  Consult now →
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.hero.label: ENS · Renewal · Updated May 2026
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.hero.title: Renewal of the ENS certificate: everything you need to know
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.hero.subtitle: The ENS certificate expires after 2 years. Many companies discover this too late and lose active contracts. This guide explains when to start the renewal, what it involves and how to avoid certification gaps.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.h2.cadaCuanto: How often does the ENS certificate need to be renewed?
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.cadaCuanto.p1: The ENS certificate has a validity of 2 years from the date of issue. This period is established in the ENS certification scheme approved by the National Cryptologic Centre (CCN) and admits no exceptions or automatic extensions.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.cadaCuanto.p2: After 2 years, the certificate expires and the organisation can no longer demonstrate its conformity with ENS. This means that, in practice, each ENS-certified company lives in a continuous cycle of two phases: implementation and first certification (once) and maintenance and periodic renewal (every two years, indefinitely).
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.cadaCuanto.p3: This biennial cadence is not arbitrary. The cybersecurity threat landscape evolves very rapidly: new vulnerabilities, new types of attacks, changes in organisations' technology architecture and updates to the ENS regulations themselves. Periodic renewal ensures that the security level remains real and up to date, not just documented.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.cadaCuanto.p4: By comparison, ISO 27001 has a validity of 3 years (with annual follow-up audits), which makes the ENS renewal cadence more demanding in terms of frequency.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.h2.queImplica: What does the ENS certificate renewal involve?
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.queImplica.p1: Renewal of the ENS certificate is not simply filling out a form or paying a fee. It involves undergoing a new full audit carried out by a certification body accredited by ENAC, similar in depth to the first certification audit.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.queImplica.li1: That the security system remains conformant with the ENS requirements for the applicable category
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.queImplica.li2: That the implemented controls remain operational and effective (not just documented)
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.queImplica.li3: That the risk analysis has been updated to reflect changes in the system, assets or threat context
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.queImplica.li4: That security incidents occurring during the certificate's validity period have been properly managed
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.queImplica.li5: That relevant technological or organisational changes (new systems, scope changes, outsourcing of services) have been managed under the ENS framework
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.queImplica.li6: That staff security training and awareness has remained active
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.queImplica.li7: That the continual improvement cycle of the security system works (internal reviews, internal audits, corrective actions)
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.queImplica.p3: If non-conformities are detected during the renewal audit, the organisation has a period to rectify them. If the non-conformities are serious, the certificate may not be renewed until they are corrected.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.h2.diferencia: Difference between renewal and new certification
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.diferencia.p1: Although the process is similar, there is an important practical difference between a renewal and a new certification from scratch.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.diferencia.p2: In a renewal, the organisation already has the security system implemented and operational. The audit starts from a state of prior conformity and verifies maintenance and continual improvement. The preparatory work is less because the controls, documentation and procedures already exist. What must be demonstrated is that they have remained active and up to date.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.diferencia.p3: In a new certification from scratch, everything must be built: risk analysis, policies, procedures, technical controls, staff training. The effort is significantly greater.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.diferencia.p4: The practical implication is that the cost of a well-maintained renewal is considerably lower than that of a first certification. However, if the organisation has neglected the maintenance of the security system during the two years of certificate validity, the renewal can be as costly as a first certification.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.cta1.text: Avson GRC offers ongoing ENS maintenance between renewals. Your certificate always up to date, no surprises.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.h2.plazos: Deadlines: when to start the renewal process
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.plazos.p1: The most frequent mistake is starting the renewal process too late. The general recommendation is to start 3-4 months before the certificate's expiry date. This deadline is necessary for several reasons:
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.plazos.li1: Updating the risk analysis and documentation takes time.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.plazos.li2: Auditors from certification bodies have schedules with weeks or months in advance. If you wait until the last days, there may be no availability on the date you need.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.plazos.li3: If non-conformities are detected in the renewal audit, you need time to rectify them before the certificate expires.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.plazos.li4: In some cases, the organisation has experienced significant changes (new systems, scope change, new technical controls) that require additional documentation and evidence work.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.timeline.m4.title: M-4 (four months before expiry)
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.timeline.m4.text: Contact the GRC consultancy and the certification body. Book audit dates. Start review of the risk analysis.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.timeline.m3.title: M-3
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.timeline.m3.text: Update the risk analysis with changes that occurred over the two years. Review and update policies, procedures and documentation. Verify that all controls remain operational.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.timeline.m2.title: M-2
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.timeline.m2.text: Perform an internal pre-audit. Identify and rectify deviations. Collect evidence of controls during the validity period.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.timeline.m1.title: M-1
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.timeline.m1.text: Renewal audit by the certification body. Resolution of any non-conformities.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.timeline.m0.title: M-0 (before expiry)
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.timeline.m0.text: Issuance of the new ENS certificate with another 2 years validity. Continuity without interruptions.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.h2.quePasa: What happens if the ENS certificate expires
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.quePasa.p1: Expiry of the ENS certificate without renewal has consequences that can be serious for the company:
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.quePasa.p2: Impossibility to demonstrate compliance: without a valid certificate, the company cannot present valid ENS conformity documentation in public tenders. This translates into direct exclusion from any contracting process that requires ENS.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.quePasa.p3: Risk in active contracts: if the company has current contracts with Public Administrations that include ENS requirements, the expiry of the certificate may constitute a contractual breach. The contracting body may require renewal under threat of penalties or contract termination.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.quePasa.p4: Loss of contract renewals: in contract renewal processes, verification of a valid ENS certificate is systematic. Expiry prevents renewal.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.quePasa.p5: Reputational impact: information on valid ENS certificates is publicly accessible in the CCN register. Clients, partners and competitors can check the certificate's status.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.quePasa.p6: If the certificate has already expired, it is not the end: the company can start a new certification process. However, depending on the time elapsed and the maintenance carried out, it can approach a first certification in effort and cost.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.mantenimiento.p1: The key to making renewal an agile and economical process is to keep the security system active throughout the certificate's validity period. Not doing so is the most costly mistake a certified company can make.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.mantenimiento.li1: Change management: any significant change in the systems in scope (new services, new suppliers, infrastructure changes, software updates) must be evaluated from the ENS security perspective and documented.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.mantenimiento.li2: Vulnerability management: for ENS Medium and High, vulnerability analysis must be performed with defined periodicity (typically quarterly or semi-annually). Findings must be managed with a remediation plan and evidence.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.mantenimiento.li3: Risk analysis update: at least annually, the risk analysis must be reviewed to reflect changes in the threat context, new assets or changes in the valuation of existing assets.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.mantenimiento.li4: Annual internal audit: although not mandatory by the ENS scheme, performing an annual internal audit is the best way to detect deviations before they become non-conformities in the renewal audit.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.mantenimiento.li5: Continuous training: personnel must receive periodic information security training and awareness, with attendance records.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.mantenimiento.li6: Incident management and logging: all security incidents must be recorded, analysed and resolved with documentation of the response. The renewal auditor will review the incident log for the period.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.h2.costes: Costs of renewing the ENS certificate
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.costes.p1: The cost of renewal has two main components:
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.costes.p2: Preparation consulting cost: if the company has kept the security system active, the preparation effort for renewal is less than that of the first certification. For a company with good maintenance, this cost is usually between 40% and 60% of the initial project cost.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.costes.p3: Renewal audit cost: the fees of the accredited certification body to carry out the renewal audit. They are similar in magnitude to those of the first audit, although in some cases they may be slightly lower if the scope has not changed and the compliance history is good.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.costes.p4: The most determining factor of renewal cost is the state of maintenance during the two years of validity. Companies that have kept the system active — updating risk analyses, managing changes, conducting internal audits — are renewed quickly and economically. Those that have neglected maintenance find renewals as costly as a first certification.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.costes.p5: Avson GRC offers ongoing ENS maintenance service: an annual subscription that ensures the security system remains up to date throughout the certificate's validity period, with quarterly reviews, change management and preparation for the renewal audit. It is the most efficient way to manage the life cycle of the ENS certificate in the long term.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.cta2.text: Lifelong relationship: we are the long-term GRC partner of our clients. Full management of the ENS certificate life cycle.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.related.title: Related articles
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.related.card1.title: ENS Medium and High: additional requirements and how to get certified
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.related.card2.title: Is the ENS certificate mandatory? Who must comply
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.faq.q1: How often does the ENS certificate need to be renewed?
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.faq.a1: The ENS certificate is valid for 2 years from the date of issue. After that period, it must be renewed through a new certification audit carried out by an ENAC-accredited body. There are no automatic extensions.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.faq.q2: What happens if the ENS certificate expires without renewal?
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.faq.a2: If the certificate expires, the company loses its ENS accreditation and cannot demonstrate compliance in new tenders. Additionally, if it has active contracts with the Public Administration that require ENS, it may incur contractual non-compliance. It is essential to start the renewal process 3-4 months in advance.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.faq.q3: Is renewal as costly as the first certification?
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.faq.a3: If the company has kept the security system active during the two years, no. The renewal cost is usually between 40% and 60% of the initial project cost. If maintenance has been poor, the renewal can approach the cost of a first certification.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.faq.q4: Can I change the certification body at renewal?
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.faq.a4: Yes. There is no obligation to renew with the same body that issued the original certificate. The company can choose any ENAC-accredited certification body for the ENS scheme. However, changing body means the new body must review all documentation from scratch, which can lengthen the process.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.faq.q5: What happens if there are non-conformities during the renewal audit?
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.faq.a5: If minor non-conformities are detected, the organisation has a period (typically 90 days) to rectify them and submit evidence of correction. If the non-conformities are major, the certificate may not be renewed until they are resolved. The certification body establishes the remediation plan.
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.faq.q6: Can the scope change at renewal?
blog/renovacion-certificado-ens.article.faq.a6: Yes. At renewal, the company can expand or reduce the scope of the certificate. If the new scope includes additional systems, the audit process will be more extensive. If the scope is reduced, it must be adequately justified. Any change of scope must be communicated to the certification body well in advance.
